## .fallowrc.json

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://json.schemastore.org/fallowrc.json",
  "ignorePatterns": [
    "dist/**",
    "node_modules/**",
    "lighthouserc.js",
    "formfiller.js",
    ".tools/**",
    "scripts/**"
  ],
  "ignoreDependencies": ["@images/*", "@astrojs/mdx", "@astrojs/sitemap", "lighthouse"],
  "rules": {
    "unused-files": "error",
    "unused-dependencies": "off",
    "unresolved-imports": "warn",
    "unlisted-dependencies": "off"
  }
}

```

```yaml
- generic [active] [ref=e1]:
  - link "Skip to main content" [ref=e2] [cursor=pointer]:

  - banner [ref=e3]:
```

```yaml
          - link "eric carlisle" [ref=e7] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: /
        - generic [ref=e8]:
          - link "Home" [ref=e9] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: /
          - link "Blog" [ref=e10] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: /blog
          - link "About" [ref=e11] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: /about
      - generic [ref=e12]:
        - button "search" [ref=e14] [cursor=pointer]
        - button "Toggle light/dark theme" [ref=e16] [cursor=pointer]:
          - img [ref=e17]
  - main [ref=e24]:
    - article [ref=e25]:
      - generic [ref=e28]:
        - generic [ref=e29]:
          - heading "About Me" [level=1] [ref=e30]
          - separator [ref=e31]
        - generic [ref=e32]:
          - generic [ref=e33]:
            - generic [ref=e34]: 15+
            - generic [ref=e35]: Years Experience
          - generic [ref=e36]:
            - generic [ref=e37]: 40+
            - generic [ref=e38]: Systems Shipped
          - generic [ref=e39]:
            - generic [ref=e40]: "99.9"
            - generic [ref=e41]: SLA P99
          - generic [ref=e42]:
            - generic [ref=e43]: "12"
            - generic [ref=e44]: Open Source
        - paragraph [ref=e45]: I'm a principal full-stack engineer and UX architect with over a decade of experience building high-performance web applications. I specialize in front-end architecture, design systems, accessibility, and modern CSS — the kind of work that makes interfaces feel intentional rather than assembled.
        - paragraph [ref=e46]: "I've spent my career straddling the line between engineering and design. That means I think in terms of systems, not pages: component libraries, design tokens, automated visual regression, custom Eleventy and Astro setups, performance budgets, and CI pipelines that catch regressions before they ship. I care about load times, color contrast ratios, keyboard flows, and what happens when a screen reader encounters a dynamically updated region."
        - paragraph [ref=e47]:
          - text: I'm also the principal behind
          - strong [ref=e48]: Eric Carlisle Consulting
          - text: ", where I partner with teams to improve their front-end infrastructure — auditing existing codebases, establishing design systems, introducing accessibility practices, and mentoring developers who want to level up their craft. Every engagement is different, but the goal is always the same: build experiences that are fast, durable, and usable by everyone."
        - paragraph [ref=e49]: This blog is where I write about what I'm learning. You'll find deep dives into web performance and Core Web Vitals, practical guides on Astro, React, and CSS architecture, notes on accessibility patterns I've encountered in the wild, and reflections on systems design — both in software and in the teams that build it.
        - paragraph [ref=e50]:
          - text: When I'm not at a keyboard, I'm usually reading, hiking, or tinkering with something that has no deadline and no delivery date. If any of this resonates, I'd love to hear from you. The best way to reach me is through
          - link "LinkedIn" [ref=e51] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://linkedin.com/in/ericcarlisle
          - text: or
          - link "GitHub" [ref=e52] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://github.com/ecarlisle
          - text: .
  - contentinfo [ref=e53]:
    - generic [ref=e56]: "STATUS: PUBLISHING · 24 PAGES LIVE"
    - text: © 2026 Eric Carlisle. All rights reserved.
    - generic [ref=e57]:
```

```yaml
          - generic [ref=e57]:
            - generic [ref=e58]: "99.9"
            - generic [ref=e59]: SLA P99
          - generic [ref=e60]:
            - generic [ref=e61]: "12"
            - generic [ref=e62]: Open Source
        - paragraph [ref=e63]: I'm a principal full-stack engineer and UX architect with over a decade of experience building high-performance web applications. I specialize in front-end architecture, design systems, accessibility, and modern CSS — the kind of work that makes interfaces feel intentional rather than assembled.
        - paragraph [ref=e64]: "I've spent my career straddling the line between engineering and design. That means I think in terms of systems, not pages: component libraries, design tokens, automated visual regression, custom Eleventy and Astro setups, performance budgets, and CI pipelines that catch regressions before they ship. I care about load times, color contrast ratios, keyboard flows, and what happens when a screen reader encounters a dynamically updated region."
        - paragraph [ref=e65]:
          - text: I'm also the principal behind
          - strong [ref=e66]: Eric Carlisle Consulting
          - text: ", where I partner with teams to improve their front-end infrastructure — auditing existing codebases, establishing design systems, introducing accessibility practices, and mentoring developers who want to level up their craft. Every engagement is different, but the goal is always the same: build experiences that are fast, durable, and usable by everyone."
        - paragraph [ref=e67]: This blog is where I write about what I'm learning. You'll find deep dives into web performance and Core Web Vitals, practical guides on Astro, React, and CSS architecture, notes on accessibility patterns I've encountered in the wild, and reflections on systems design — both in software and in the teams that build it.
        - paragraph [ref=e68]:
          - text: When I'm not at a keyboard, I'm usually reading, hiking, or tinkering with something that has no deadline and no delivery date. If any of this resonates, I'd love to hear from you. The best way to reach me is through
          - link "LinkedIn" [ref=e69] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://linkedin.com/in/ericcarlisle
          - text: or
          - link "GitHub" [ref=e70] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://github.com/ecarlisle
          - text: .
  - contentinfo [ref=e71]:

            - /url: https://bsky.app/profile/ericcarlisle.bsky.social
```

```yaml
            - img [ref=e37]
          - link "Go to Eric Carlisle's Mastodon profile (opens in new tab)" [ref=e39] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://fosstodon.org/@ericcarlisle
            - generic [ref=e40]: Go to Eric Carlisle's Mastodon profile (opens in new tab)
            - img [ref=e41]
  - main [ref=e43]:
    - link "← Back to all posts" [ref=e44] [cursor=pointer]:
      - /url: /blog
    - article [ref=e45]:
      - img "Building Forms Everyone Can Use" [ref=e47]
      - generic [ref=e48]:
        - generic [ref=e49]:
          - generic [ref=e50]:
            - generic [ref=e51]:
              - generic [ref=e52]:
                - time [ref=e53]: Aug 12, 2022
                - generic [ref=e54]: ·1 min read
              - heading "Building Forms Everyone Can Use" [level=1] [ref=e55]
              - separator [ref=e56]
            - paragraph [ref=e57]: Forms are the most common interaction on the web, and they’re also where accessibility failures cluster. The good news is that fixing them doesn’t require heroics — just attention to a few patterns.
            - heading "Label Everything" [level=2] [ref=e58]
            - paragraph [ref=e59]:
              - text: Every input needs a
              - code [ref=e60]: <label>
              - text: . Placeholders are not labels — they disappear on input, leaving users with no context.
            - figure [ref=e62]:
              - code [ref=e64]:
                - generic [ref=e66]: <label for="email">Email address</label>
                - generic [ref=e68]: <input type="email" id="email" name="email" autocomplete="email" />
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e70] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Error Messages That Work" [level=2] [ref=e72]
            - paragraph [ref=e73]: "Inline validation is best, but it needs to be connected to its input programmatically:"
            - figure [ref=e75]:
              - code [ref=e77]:
                - generic [ref=e79]: <label for="name">Full name</label>
                - generic [ref=e81]: <input
                - generic [ref=e83]: type="text"
                - generic [ref=e85]: id="name"
                - generic [ref=e87]: name="name"
                - generic [ref=e89]: aria-describedby="name-error"
                - generic [ref=e91]: aria-invalid="true"
                - generic [ref=e93]: />
                - generic [ref=e95]: <span id="name-error" role="alert">Name is required.</span>
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e97] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e99]:
              - code [ref=e100]: aria-describedby
              - text: ties the error to the input so screen readers announce it when focusing.
              - code [ref=e101]: role="alert"
              - text: makes the error message interrupt politely.
            - heading "Focus Management After Submission" [level=2] [ref=e102]
            - paragraph [ref=e103]: After a form submission with errors, move focus to the first invalid input. This prevents keyboard users from having to tab through the entire form again to find what went wrong.
            - figure [ref=e105]:
              - code [ref=e107]:
                - generic [ref=e109]: const firstError = form.querySelector('[aria-invalid="true"]');
                - generic [ref=e111]: if (firstError) firstError.focus();
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e113] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Testing" [level=2] [ref=e115]
            - paragraph [ref=e116]: Run your form through axe DevTools, then navigate it with a keyboard. If you can complete the flow with only Tab, Enter, and Escape, you’re 90% of the way there.
          - paragraph [ref=e118]:
            - link "accessibility" [ref=e119] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /tags/accessibility/
            - link "engineering" [ref=e120] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /tags/engineering/
          - navigation "Pagination" [ref=e121]:
            - link "← Previous CSS Container Queries in Practice" [ref=e122] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /blog/css-container-queries/
              - generic [ref=e123]: ← Previous
              - text: CSS Container Queries in Practice
            - 'link "Next → Astro View Transitions: A Practical Walkthrough" [ref=e124] [cursor=pointer]':
              - /url: /blog/astro-view-transitions/
              - generic [ref=e125]: Next →
              - text: "Astro View Transitions: A Practical Walkthrough"
        - complementary [ref=e126]:
          - generic [ref=e128]:
            - generic [ref=e129]: Share
```

```yaml
            - img [ref=e41]
  - main [ref=e43]:
    - article [ref=e44]:
      - generic [ref=e47]:
        - generic [ref=e48]:
          - time [ref=e50]: Aug 8, 2021
          - heading "About Me" [level=1] [ref=e51]
          - separator [ref=e52]
        - generic [ref=e53]:
          - generic [ref=e54]:
            - generic [ref=e55]: 15+
            - generic [ref=e56]: Years Experience
          - generic [ref=e57]:
            - generic [ref=e58]: 40+
            - generic [ref=e59]: Systems Shipped
          - generic [ref=e60]:
            - generic [ref=e61]: "99.9"
            - generic [ref=e62]: SLA P99
          - generic [ref=e63]:
            - generic [ref=e64]: "12"
            - generic [ref=e65]: Open Source
        - paragraph [ref=e66]: I'm a principal full-stack engineer and UX architect with over a decade of experience building high-performance web applications. I specialize in front-end architecture, design systems, accessibility, and modern CSS — the kind of work that makes interfaces feel intentional rather than assembled.
        - paragraph [ref=e67]: "I've spent my career straddling the line between engineering and design. That means I think in terms of systems, not pages: component libraries, design tokens, automated visual regression, custom Eleventy and Astro setups, performance budgets, and CI pipelines that catch regressions before they ship. I care about load times, color contrast ratios, keyboard flows, and what happens when a screen reader encounters a dynamically updated region."
        - paragraph [ref=e68]:
          - text: I'm also the principal behind
          - strong [ref=e69]: Eric Carlisle Consulting
          - text: ", where I partner with teams to improve their front-end infrastructure — auditing existing codebases, establishing design systems, introducing accessibility practices, and mentoring developers who want to level up their craft. Every engagement is different, but the goal is always the same: build experiences that are fast, durable, and usable by everyone."
        - paragraph [ref=e70]: This blog is where I write about what I'm learning. You'll find deep dives into web performance and Core Web Vitals, practical guides on Astro, React, and CSS architecture, notes on accessibility patterns I've encountered in the wild, and reflections on systems design — both in software and in the teams that build it.
        - paragraph [ref=e71]:
          - text: When I'm not at a keyboard, I'm usually reading, hiking, or tinkering with something that has no deadline and no delivery date. If any of this resonates, I'd love to hear from you. The best way to reach me is through
          - link "LinkedIn" [ref=e72] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://linkedin.com/in/ericcarlisle
          - text: or
          - link "GitHub" [ref=e73] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://github.com/ecarlisle
          - text: .
  - contentinfo [ref=e74]:
```

```yaml
  - main [ref=e43]:
    - link "← Back to all posts" [ref=e44] [cursor=pointer]:
      - /url: /blog
    - article [ref=e45]:
      - img "Building Forms Everyone Can Use" [ref=e47]
      - generic [ref=e48]:
        - generic [ref=e49]:
          - generic [ref=e50]:
            - generic [ref=e51]:
              - generic [ref=e52]:
                - time [ref=e53]: Aug 12, 2022
                - generic [ref=e54]: ·1 min read
              - heading "Building Forms Everyone Can Use" [level=1] [ref=e55]
              - separator [ref=e56]
            - paragraph [ref=e57]: Forms are the most common interaction on the web, and they’re also where accessibility failures cluster. The good news is that fixing them doesn’t require heroics — just attention to a few patterns.
            - heading "Label Everything" [level=2] [ref=e58]
            - paragraph [ref=e59]:
              - text: Every input needs a
              - code [ref=e60]: <label>
              - text: . Placeholders are not labels — they disappear on input, leaving users with no context.
            - figure [ref=e62]:
              - code [ref=e64]:
                - generic [ref=e66]: <label for="email">Email address</label>
                - generic [ref=e68]: <input type="email" id="email" name="email" autocomplete="email" />
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e70] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Error Messages That Work" [level=2] [ref=e72]
            - paragraph [ref=e73]: "Inline validation is best, but it needs to be connected to its input programmatically:"
            - figure [ref=e75]:
              - code [ref=e77]:
                - generic [ref=e79]: <label for="name">Full name</label>
                - generic [ref=e81]: <input
                - generic [ref=e83]: type="text"
                - generic [ref=e85]: id="name"
                - generic [ref=e87]: name="name"
                - generic [ref=e89]: aria-describedby="name-error"
                - generic [ref=e91]: aria-invalid="true"
                - generic [ref=e93]: />
                - generic [ref=e95]: <span id="name-error" role="alert">Name is required.</span>
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e97] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e99]:
              - code [ref=e100]: aria-describedby
              - text: ties the error to the input so screen readers announce it when focusing.
              - code [ref=e101]: role="alert"
              - text: makes the error message interrupt politely.
            - heading "Focus Management After Submission" [level=2] [ref=e102]
            - paragraph [ref=e103]: After a form submission with errors, move focus to the first invalid input. This prevents keyboard users from having to tab through the entire form again to find what went wrong.
            - figure [ref=e105]:
              - code [ref=e107]:
                - generic [ref=e109]: const firstError = form.querySelector('[aria-invalid="true"]');
                - generic [ref=e111]: if (firstError) firstError.focus();
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e113] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Testing" [level=2] [ref=e115]
            - paragraph [ref=e116]: Run your form through axe DevTools, then navigate it with a keyboard. If you can complete the flow with only Tab, Enter, and Escape, you’re 90% of the way there.
          - paragraph [ref=e118]:
            - link "accessibility" [ref=e119] [cursor=pointer]:

            - link "engineering" [ref=e120] [cursor=pointer]:

            - 'link "Next → Astro View Transitions: A Practical Walkthrough" [ref=e124] [cursor=pointer]':
```

```yaml
            - generic [ref=e40]: Go to Eric Carlisle's Mastodon profile (opens in new tab)
            - img [ref=e41]
  - main [ref=e43]:
    - link "← Back to all posts" [ref=e44] [cursor=pointer]:
      - /url: /blog
    - article [ref=e45]:
      - 'img "Core Web Vitals: Beyond the Dashboard" [ref=e47]'
      - generic [ref=e48]:
        - generic [ref=e49]:
          - generic [ref=e50]:
            - generic [ref=e51]:
              - generic [ref=e52]:
                - time [ref=e53]: Sep 9, 2022
                - generic [ref=e54]: ·2 min read
              - 'heading "Core Web Vitals: Beyond the Dashboard" [level=1] [ref=e55]'
              - separator [ref=e56]
            - paragraph [ref=e57]: The Core Web Vitals dashboard tells you what’s slow, but not why. Bridging that gap requires understanding what each metric actually measures and how the browser computes it.
            - 'heading "LCP: Largest Contentful Paint" [level=2] [ref=e58]'
            - paragraph [ref=e59]: The most common culprit is the hero image. But not always — LCP can also be a text block rendered with a web font that loads late.
            - paragraph [ref=e60]: "For images, preload the LCP candidate:"
            - figure [ref=e62]:
              - code [ref=e64]:
                - generic [ref=e66]: <link rel="preload" as="image" href="/hero.webp" fetchpriority="high" />
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e68] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e70]:
              - text: For text, ensure your font is self-hosted, preloaded, and uses
              - code [ref=e71]: "font-display: swap"
              - text: with a matching fallback.
            - 'heading "CLS: Cumulative Layout Shift" [level=2] [ref=e72]'
            - paragraph [ref=e73]: "CLS happens when elements move after the user has started interacting. The most common sources:"
            - list [ref=e74]:
              - listitem [ref=e75]: Images without dimensions — always set width and height
              - listitem [ref=e76]: Ads and embeds that reserve no space
              - listitem [ref=e77]: Web fonts causing a text re-layout (FOUT/FOIT)
            - paragraph [ref=e78]:
              - text: "The fix for images is simple and has been standard advice for years: always set"
              - code [ref=e79]: width
              - text: and
              - code [ref=e80]: height
              - text: on images, or use
              - code [ref=e81]: aspect-ratio
              - text: in CSS.
            - 'heading "INP: Interaction to Next Frame" [level=2] [ref=e82]'
            - paragraph [ref=e83]:
              - text: INP measures the delay between a user interaction and the next frame being painted. Long tasks are the enemy here. Break up JavaScript-heavy interactions with
              - code [ref=e84]: setTimeout
              - text: or
              - code [ref=e85]: scheduler.yield()
              - text: to let the browser paint between chunks.
            - heading "Measuring in the Field" [level=2] [ref=e86]
            - paragraph [ref=e87]: Lab data (Lighthouse) catches regressions. Field data (CrUX, RUM) catches real user pain. You need both. A fast lab score with poor field data means your testing doesn’t match your users’ reality — usually slower devices or networks you’re not emulating.
          - button "performance" [ref=e90] [cursor=pointer]
          - navigation "Pagination" [ref=e91]:
            - link "← Previous Design Token Workflows at Scale" [ref=e92] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /blog/design-token-workflows/
              - generic [ref=e93]: ← Previous
              - text: Design Token Workflows at Scale
            - link "Next → A Modern CSS Reset for 2024" [ref=e94] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /blog/modern-css-reset/
              - generic [ref=e95]: Next →
              - text: A Modern CSS Reset for 2024
        - complementary [ref=e96]:
          - generic [ref=e97]:
            - generic [ref=e99]:
              - generic [ref=e100]: Share
              - generic [ref=e101]:
                - link "Share on X / Twitter" [ref=e102] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=Core%20Web%20Vitals%3A%20Beyond%20the%20Dashboard&url=https%3A%2F%2Fericcarlisle.com%2Fblog%2Fcore-web-vitals%2F
                  - img [ref=e103]
                - link "Share on LinkedIn" [ref=e105] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fericcarlisle.com%2Fblog%2Fcore-web-vitals%2F

                - link "Share on Bluesky" [ref=e111] [cursor=pointer]:

                  - img [ref=e112]
            - generic [ref=e114]:
              - heading "On this page" [level=3] [ref=e115]
              - navigation "Table of contents" [ref=e116]:
                - list [ref=e117]:
                  - listitem [ref=e118]:
                    - 'link "LCP: Largest Contentful Paint" [ref=e119] [cursor=pointer]':
                      - /url: "#lcp-largest-contentful-paint"
                  - listitem [ref=e120]:
                    - 'link "CLS: Cumulative Layout Shift" [ref=e121] [cursor=pointer]':
```

## .playwright-mcp/page-2026-06-20T20-05-54-761Z.yml

```yaml
- generic [active] [ref=e1]:
  - progressbar "Reading progress"
  - link "Skip to main content" [ref=e2] [cursor=pointer]:
    - /url: "#main-content"
  - banner [ref=e3]:
    - generic [ref=e4]:
      - navigation "Main navigation" [ref=e5]:
        - heading "eric carlisle" [level=2] [ref=e6]:
          - link "eric carlisle" [ref=e7] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: /
        - generic [ref=e8]:
          - link "Home" [ref=e9] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: /
          - link "Blog" [ref=e10] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: /blog
          - link "About" [ref=e11] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: /about
          - link "Contact" [ref=e12] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: /contact
      - generic [ref=e13]:
        - button "search" [ref=e15] [cursor=pointer]
        - button "Toggle light/dark theme" [ref=e17] [cursor=pointer]:
          - img [ref=e18]
        - generic [ref=e26]:
          - link "Go to Eric Carlisle's GitHub profile (opens in new tab)" [ref=e27] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://github.com/ecarlisle
            - generic [ref=e28]: Go to Eric Carlisle's GitHub profile (opens in new tab)
            - img [ref=e29]
          - link "Go to Eric Carlisle's LinkedIn profile (opens in new tab)" [ref=e31] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ericcarlisle/
            - generic [ref=e32]: Go to Eric Carlisle's LinkedIn profile (opens in new tab)
            - img [ref=e33]
          - link "Go to Eric Carlisle's Bluesky profile (opens in new tab)" [ref=e35] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://bsky.app/profile/ericcarlisle.bsky.social
            - generic [ref=e36]: Go to Eric Carlisle's Bluesky profile (opens in new tab)
            - img [ref=e37]
          - link "Go to Eric Carlisle's Mastodon profile (opens in new tab)" [ref=e39] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://fosstodon.org/@ericcarlisle
            - generic [ref=e40]: Go to Eric Carlisle's Mastodon profile (opens in new tab)
            - img [ref=e41]
  - main [ref=e43]:
    - link "← Back to all posts" [ref=e44] [cursor=pointer]:
      - /url: /blog
    - article [ref=e45]:
      - img "Design Tokens on This Site" [ref=e47]
      - generic [ref=e48]:
        - generic [ref=e49]:
          - generic [ref=e50]:
            - generic [ref=e51]:
              - generic [ref=e52]:
                - time [ref=e53]: Jun 16, 2026
                - generic [ref=e54]: ·3 min read
              - heading "Design Tokens on This Site" [level=1] [ref=e55]
              - separator [ref=e56]
            - paragraph [ref=e57]:
              - text: "Every visual decision on this site flows through CSS custom properties defined in a single file:"
              - code [ref=e58]: src/styles/global.css
              - text: . This article documents those tokens, their relationships, and the rationale behind them.
            - heading "Typography" [level=2] [ref=e59]
            - heading "Font Families" [level=3] [ref=e60]
            - figure [ref=e62]:
              - code [ref=e64]:
                - generic [ref=e66]: "--font-copy: \"Inter Variable\", system-ui, sans-serif;"
                - generic [ref=e68]: "--font-headers: \"Plus Jakarta Sans Variable\", system-ui, sans-serif;"
                - generic [ref=e70]: "--font-mono: \"Fira Code Variable\", ui-monospace, monospace;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e72] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e74]: Inter is the workhorse body face — narrow, tall x-height, excellent at small sizes. Plus Jakarta Sans handles headings with a more geometric, open character. Fira Code provides monospace with ligatures.
            - heading "Type Size Scale" [level=3] [ref=e75]
            - paragraph [ref=e76]:
              - text: Every size is fluid — it scales smoothly between viewport widths using
              - code [ref=e77]: clamp()
              - text: ":"
            - figure [ref=e79]:
              - code [ref=e81]:
                - generic [ref=e83]: "--type-size-body: clamp(1rem, 0.45vw + 0.91rem, 1.25rem); /* 16px → 20px */"
                - generic [ref=e85]: "--type-size-small: clamp(0.8125rem, 0.2vw + 0.77rem, 0.9375rem); /* 13px → 15px */"
                - generic [ref=e87]: "--type-size-h1: clamp(2rem, 3.64vw + 1.27rem, 4rem); /* 32px → 64px */"
                - generic [ref=e89]: "--type-size-h2: clamp(1.625rem, 2.13vw + 1.2rem, 2.8rem); /* 26px → 45px */"
                - generic [ref=e91]: "--type-size-h3: clamp(1.375rem, 1.14vw + 1.15rem, 2rem); /* 22px → 32px */"
                - generic [ref=e93]: "--type-size-h4: clamp(1.125rem, 0.57vw + 1.07rem, 1.5rem); /* 18px → 24px */"
                - generic [ref=e95]: "--type-size-h5: clamp(1rem, 0.28vw + 0.94rem, 1.1875rem); /* 16px → 19px */"
                - generic [ref=e97]: "--type-size-blockquote: 1.1em;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e99] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e101]:
              - text: The scale uses a roughly 1.25 modular ratio (major third). The
              - code [ref=e102]: clamp()
              - text: endpoints are chosen so that body text never drops below 16 px (the minimum for comfortable long-form reading on a 13″ laptop) and never exceeds it by more than 25 %.
            - heading "Line Heights" [level=3] [ref=e103]
            - figure [ref=e105]:
              - code [ref=e107]:
                - generic [ref=e109]: "--line-height-body: 1.65; /* body copy — generous for readability */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e111] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e113]: There is no separate heading line-height token. Each heading level sets its own line-height as a multiple of the vertical rhythm unit (see below).
            - heading "Vertical Rhythm" [level=2] [ref=e114]
            - paragraph [ref=e115]: "The entire vertical spacing system is anchored to one value:"
            - figure [ref=e117]:
              - code [ref=e119]:
                - generic [ref=e121]: "--rhythm: calc(var(--line-height-body) * 1rem); /* ≈ 1.65 rem */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e123] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e125]:
              - code [ref=e126]: "--rhythm"
              - text: equals one line of body text. Every margin, padding, and heading line-height is a clean multiple of this unit, creating a consistent baseline grid.
            - heading "Heading Sizes and Spacing" [level=3] [ref=e127]
            - table [ref=e128]:
              - rowgroup [ref=e129]:
                - row "Heading Font size (fluid) Line-height (× rhythm) Top margin Bottom margin" [ref=e130]:
                  - columnheader "Heading" [ref=e131]
                  - columnheader "Font size (fluid)" [ref=e132]
                  - columnheader "Line-height (× rhythm)" [ref=e133]
                  - columnheader "Top margin" [ref=e134]
                  - columnheader "Bottom margin" [ref=e135]
              - rowgroup [ref=e136]:
                - row "h1 32 → 64 px 3× (≈ 79 px) 0 1× (≈ 26 px)" [ref=e137]:
                  - cell "h1" [ref=e138]
                  - cell "32 → 64 px" [ref=e139]
                  - cell "3× (≈ 79 px)" [ref=e140]
                  - cell "0" [ref=e141]
                  - cell "1× (≈ 26 px)" [ref=e142]
                - row "h2 26 → 45 px 2× (≈ 53 px) 2× 1×" [ref=e143]:
                  - cell "h2" [ref=e144]
                  - cell "26 → 45 px" [ref=e145]
                  - cell "2× (≈ 53 px)" [ref=e146]
                  - cell "2×" [ref=e147]
                  - cell "1×" [ref=e148]
                - row "h3 22 → 32 px 2× (≈ 53 px) 1.5× 0.5×" [ref=e149]:
                  - cell "h3" [ref=e150]
                  - cell "22 → 32 px" [ref=e151]
                  - cell "2× (≈ 53 px)" [ref=e152]
                  - cell "1.5×" [ref=e153]
                  - cell "0.5×" [ref=e154]
                - row "h4 18 → 24 px 1.5× (≈ 40 px) 1.5× 0.5×" [ref=e155]:
                  - cell "h4" [ref=e156]
                  - cell "18 → 24 px" [ref=e157]
                  - cell "1.5× (≈ 40 px)" [ref=e158]
                  - cell "1.5×" [ref=e159]
                  - cell "0.5×" [ref=e160]
                - row "h5 16 → 19 px 1.25× (≈ 33 px) 1× 0.5×" [ref=e161]:
                  - cell "h5" [ref=e162]
                  - cell "16 → 19 px" [ref=e163]
                  - cell "1.25× (≈ 33 px)" [ref=e164]
                  - cell "1×" [ref=e165]
                  - cell "0.5×" [ref=e166]
            - paragraph [ref=e167]: The bottom margin of a heading is always smaller than its top margin, so each heading visually attaches to the content below it rather than floating between sections.
            - heading "Spacing Scale" [level=2] [ref=e168]
            - paragraph [ref=e169]:
              - text: The spacing tokens provide the building blocks for layout, padding, and gaps. Every value is a multiple of
              - code [ref=e170]: "--rhythm"
              - text: ":"
            - figure [ref=e172]:
              - code [ref=e174]:
                - generic [ref=e176]: "--space-xs: calc(var(--rhythm) * 0.5); /* 0.5× ≈ 13 px */"
                - generic [ref=e178]: "--space-sm: calc(var(--rhythm) * 0.75); /* 0.75× ≈ 20 px */"
                - generic [ref=e180]: "--space-md: calc(var(--rhythm) * 1); /* 1× ≈ 26 px */"
                - generic [ref=e182]: "--space-lg: calc(var(--rhythm) * 1.5); /* 1.5× ≈ 40 px */"
                - generic [ref=e184]: "--space-xl: calc(var(--rhythm) * 2); /* 2× ≈ 53 px */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e186] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e188]: "These are further aliased by purpose so that changing one token adjusts spacing everywhere it is used:"
            - figure [ref=e190]:
              - code [ref=e192]:
                - generic [ref=e194]: "--space-inline: var(--space-xs); /* tight inline gaps (tag spacing, icon gaps) */"
                - generic [ref=e196]: "--space-inline-strong: var(--space-sm); /* stronger inline separation (card children) */"
                - generic [ref=e198]: "--space-component: var(--space-md); /* default component padding */"
                - generic [ref=e200]: "--space-component-lg: var(--space-lg); /* large component padding */"
                - generic [ref=e202]: "--space-section: var(--space-lg); /* section-level margins (headings, blockquotes) */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e204] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Color Palette" [level=2] [ref=e206]
            - paragraph [ref=e207]: The colors use the OKLCH color space for perceptual uniformity. The default theme is a warm dark mode with an amber/coral editorial palette.
            - paragraph [ref=e208]:
              - text: "Both override mechanisms are supported: the"
              - code [ref=e209]: "[data-theme=\"light\"]"
              - text: attribute selector for the JS theme toggle, and
              - code [ref=e210]: "@media (prefers-color-scheme: light)"
              - text: for system-level preference. They hold identical values, so the light appearance is the same regardless of which trigger fires.
            - heading "Dark Theme (default)" [level=3] [ref=e211]
            - figure [ref=e213]:
              - code [ref=e215]:
                - generic [ref=e217]: "--bg-main: oklch(0.12 0.015 280); /* deep navy-black background */"
                - generic [ref=e219]: "--bg-surface: oklch(0.16 0.02 280); /* card / surface background */"
                - generic [ref=e221]: "--bg-surface-elevated: oklch(0.2 0.025 280); /* elevated surface (hovered cards) */"
                - generic [ref=e225]: "--text-primary: oklch(0.95 0.008 280); /* main body text — 15.05:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e227]: "--text-secondary: oklch(0.77 0.015 280); /* secondary text — 4.50:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e229]: "--text-muted: oklch(0.72 0.02 280); /* muted / meta text — 3.28:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e233]: "--brand-primary: oklch(0.64 0.16 75); /* Primary Amber */"
                - generic [ref=e235]: "--brand-accent: oklch(0.58 0.18 35); /* Accent Coral */"
                - generic [ref=e237]: "--brand-highlight: oklch(0.81 0.15 110); /* Highlight Chartreuse */"
                - generic [ref=e239]: "--neutral-anchor: oklch(0.53 0.08 230); /* Anchor Slate */"
                - generic [ref=e241]: "--accent-cold: oklch(0.69 0.15 250); /* Interaction Cerulean */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e243] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Light Theme Override" [level=3] [ref=e245]
            - paragraph [ref=e246]: "The light theme is triggered by either the attribute selector or the system preference media query, and overrides only the color tokens. Typography and spacing stay the same:"
            - figure [ref=e248]:
              - code [ref=e250]:
                - generic [ref=e252]: "[data-theme=\"light\"] {"
                - generic [ref=e254]: "--bg-main: oklch(0.985 0.003 280); /* near-white background */"
                - generic [ref=e256]: "--bg-surface: oklch(1 0 0); /* pure white cards */"
                - generic [ref=e258]: "--bg-surface-elevated: oklch(0.92 0.008 280);"
                - generic [ref=e262]: "--text-primary: oklch(0.13 0.015 280);"
                - generic [ref=e264]: "--text-secondary: oklch(0.25 0.02 280);"
                - generic [ref=e266]: "--text-muted: oklch(0.38 0.025 280);"
                - generic [ref=e270]: "--brand-primary: oklch(0.55 0.15 75); /* Rich Amber */"
                - generic [ref=e272]: "--brand-accent: oklch(0.48 0.16 35); /* Deep Coral */"
                - generic [ref=e274]: "--brand-highlight: oklch(0.45 0.14 110); /* High-Contrast Chartreuse */"
                - generic [ref=e276]: "--neutral-anchor: oklch(0.35 0.06 230); /* Dark Slate Anchor */"
                - generic [ref=e278]: "--accent-cold: oklch(0.52 0.14 250); /* Deep Interactive Cerulean */"
                - generic [ref=e280]: "}"
                - generic [ref=e284]: "@media (prefers-color-scheme: light) {"
                - generic [ref=e286]: ":root { /* identical values */ }"
                - generic [ref=e288]: "}"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e290] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e292]: Using OKLCH ensures that the light and dark themes stay perceptually consistent — the same hue angles produce recognizably the same color families across themes, differing only in lightness. Every text token on both themes exceeds WCAG AA contrast against its background.
            - heading "Border Radii" [level=2] [ref=e293]
            - figure [ref=e295]:
              - code [ref=e297]:
                - generic [ref=e299]: "--radius-sm: 4px;"
                - generic [ref=e301]: "--radius-md: 8px;"
                - generic [ref=e303]: "--radius-lg: 12px;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e305] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e307]:
              - text: A tight radius system. Cards use
              - code [ref=e308]: "--radius-lg"
              - text: ", buttons and inputs use"
              - code [ref=e309]: "--radius-md"
              - text: ", code blocks use"
              - code [ref=e310]: "--radius-sm"
              - text: .
            - heading "Motion" [level=2] [ref=e311]
            - figure [ref=e313]:
              - code [ref=e315]:
                - generic [ref=e317]: "--transition-fast: 0.15s ease-out;"
                - generic [ref=e319]: "--transition-normal: 0.25s ease-out;"
                - generic [ref=e321]: "--transition-slow: 0.4s ease-out;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e323] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e325]:
              - text: All transitions use
              - code [ref=e326]: ease-out
              - text: — fast in, slow out — which feels responsive because the motion starts immediately and coasts to rest. The
              - code [ref=e327]: "prefers-reduced-motion: reduce"
              - text: media query disables all transitions and animations.
            - heading "Structural Tokens" [level=2] [ref=e328]
            - figure [ref=e330]:
              - code [ref=e332]:
                - generic [ref=e334]: "--size-border-pixel: 2px;"
                - generic [ref=e336]: "--size-border-thick: 4px;"
                - generic [ref=e338]: "--size-touch-target-min: 44px;"
                - generic [ref=e342]: "--layout-max-width: 68ch; /* comfortable reading measure */"
                - generic [ref=e344]: "--layout-max-width-wide: 75rem; /* full page content area */"
                - generic [ref=e346]: "--bp-mobile: 720px; /* mobile breakpoint */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e348] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e350]:
              - text: The 68-character line length for body text follows the established range of 45–75 characters per line. The touch target minimum of 44px matches
              - link "WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 2.5.8" [ref=e351] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/#target-size
              - text: .
          - generic [ref=e353]:
            - button "css" [ref=e354] [cursor=pointer]
            - button "design-systems" [ref=e355] [cursor=pointer]
          - navigation "Pagination" [ref=e356]:
            - link "← Previous Markdown Style Guide" [ref=e357] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /blog/markdown-style-guide/
```

```yaml
            - generic [ref=e40]: Go to Eric Carlisle's Mastodon profile (opens in new tab)
            - img [ref=e41]
  - main [ref=e43]:
    - link "← Back to all posts" [ref=e44] [cursor=pointer]:
      - /url: /blog
    - article [ref=e45]:
      - 'img "Core Web Vitals: Beyond the Dashboard" [ref=e47]'
      - generic [ref=e48]:
        - generic [ref=e49]:
          - generic [ref=e50]:
            - generic [ref=e51]:
              - generic [ref=e52]:
                - time [ref=e53]: Sep 9, 2022
                - generic [ref=e54]: ·2 min read
              - 'heading "Core Web Vitals: Beyond the Dashboard" [level=1] [ref=e55]'
              - separator [ref=e56]
            - paragraph [ref=e57]: The Core Web Vitals dashboard tells you what’s slow, but not why. Bridging that gap requires understanding what each metric actually measures and how the browser computes it.
            - 'heading "LCP: Largest Contentful Paint" [level=2] [ref=e58]'
            - paragraph [ref=e59]: The most common culprit is the hero image. But not always — LCP can also be a text block rendered with a web font that loads late.
            - paragraph [ref=e60]: "For images, preload the LCP candidate:"
            - figure [ref=e62]:
              - code [ref=e64]:
                - generic [ref=e66]: <link rel="preload" as="image" href="/hero.webp" fetchpriority="high" />
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e68] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e70]:
              - text: For text, ensure your font is self-hosted, preloaded, and uses
              - code [ref=e71]: "font-display: swap"
              - text: with a matching fallback.
            - 'heading "CLS: Cumulative Layout Shift" [level=2] [ref=e72]'
            - paragraph [ref=e73]: "CLS happens when elements move after the user has started interacting. The most common sources:"
            - list [ref=e74]:
              - listitem [ref=e75]: Images without dimensions — always set width and height
              - listitem [ref=e76]: Ads and embeds that reserve no space
              - listitem [ref=e77]: Web fonts causing a text re-layout (FOUT/FOIT)
            - paragraph [ref=e78]:
              - text: "The fix for images is simple and has been standard advice for years: always set"
              - code [ref=e79]: width
              - text: and
              - code [ref=e80]: height
              - text: on images, or use
              - code [ref=e81]: aspect-ratio
              - text: in CSS.
            - 'heading "INP: Interaction to Next Frame" [level=2] [ref=e82]'
            - paragraph [ref=e83]:
              - text: INP measures the delay between a user interaction and the next frame being painted. Long tasks are the enemy here. Break up JavaScript-heavy interactions with
              - code [ref=e84]: setTimeout
              - text: or
              - code [ref=e85]: scheduler.yield()
              - text: to let the browser paint between chunks.
            - heading "Measuring in the Field" [level=2] [ref=e86]
            - paragraph [ref=e87]: Lab data (Lighthouse) catches regressions. Field data (CrUX, RUM) catches real user pain. You need both. A fast lab score with poor field data means your testing doesn’t match your users’ reality — usually slower devices or networks you’re not emulating.
          - generic [ref=e88]:
            - group "Filter posts by tag" [ref=e89]:
              - button "All" [ref=e90] [cursor=pointer]
```

```yaml
              - 'heading "Core Web Vitals: Beyond the Dashboard" [level=1] [ref=e55]'
              - separator [ref=e56]
            - paragraph [ref=e57]: The Core Web Vitals dashboard tells you what’s slow, but not why. Bridging that gap requires understanding what each metric actually measures and how the browser computes it.
            - 'heading "LCP: Largest Contentful Paint" [level=2] [ref=e58]'
            - paragraph [ref=e59]: The most common culprit is the hero image. But not always — LCP can also be a text block rendered with a web font that loads late.
            - paragraph [ref=e60]: "For images, preload the LCP candidate:"
            - figure [ref=e62]:
              - code [ref=e64]:
                - generic [ref=e66]: <link rel="preload" as="image" href="/hero.webp" fetchpriority="high" />
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e68] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e70]:
              - text: For text, ensure your font is self-hosted, preloaded, and uses
              - code [ref=e71]: "font-display: swap"
              - text: with a matching fallback.
            - 'heading "CLS: Cumulative Layout Shift" [level=2] [ref=e72]'
            - paragraph [ref=e73]: "CLS happens when elements move after the user has started interacting. The most common sources:"
            - list [ref=e74]:
              - listitem [ref=e75]: Images without dimensions — always set width and height
              - listitem [ref=e76]: Ads and embeds that reserve no space
              - listitem [ref=e77]: Web fonts causing a text re-layout (FOUT/FOIT)
            - paragraph [ref=e78]:
              - text: "The fix for images is simple and has been standard advice for years: always set"
              - code [ref=e79]: width
              - text: and
              - code [ref=e80]: height
              - text: on images, or use
              - code [ref=e81]: aspect-ratio
              - text: in CSS.
            - 'heading "INP: Interaction to Next Frame" [level=2] [ref=e82]'
            - paragraph [ref=e83]:
              - text: INP measures the delay between a user interaction and the next frame being painted. Long tasks are the enemy here. Break up JavaScript-heavy interactions with
              - code [ref=e84]: setTimeout
              - text: or
              - code [ref=e85]: scheduler.yield()
              - text: to let the browser paint between chunks.
            - heading "Measuring in the Field" [level=2] [ref=e86]
            - paragraph [ref=e87]: Lab data (Lighthouse) catches regressions. Field data (CrUX, RUM) catches real user pain. You need both. A fast lab score with poor field data means your testing doesn’t match your users’ reality — usually slower devices or networks you’re not emulating.
          - button "performance 3" [ref=e90] [cursor=pointer]:
            - text: performance
            - superscript [ref=e91]: "3"
          - navigation "Pagination" [ref=e92]:
            - link "← Previous Design Token Workflows at Scale" [ref=e93] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /blog/design-token-workflows/
              - generic [ref=e94]: ← Previous
              - text: Design Token Workflows at Scale
            - link "Next → A Modern CSS Reset for 2024" [ref=e95] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /blog/modern-css-reset/
              - generic [ref=e96]: Next →
              - text: A Modern CSS Reset for 2024
        - complementary [ref=e97]:
          - generic [ref=e98]:
            - generic [ref=e100]:
              - generic [ref=e101]: Share
              - generic [ref=e102]:
                - link "Share on X / Twitter" [ref=e103] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=Core%20Web%20Vitals%3A%20Beyond%20the%20Dashboard&url=https%3A%2F%2Fericcarlisle.com%2Fblog%2Fcore-web-vitals%2F
                  - img [ref=e104]
                - link "Share on LinkedIn" [ref=e106] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fericcarlisle.com%2Fblog%2Fcore-web-vitals%2F
                  - img [ref=e107]
                - link "Share on Mastodon" [ref=e109] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: https://toot.kytta.dev/?text=Core%20Web%20Vitals%3A%20Beyond%20the%20Dashboard%20https%3A%2F%2Fericcarlisle.com%2Fblog%2Fcore-web-vitals%2F
                  - img [ref=e110]
                - link "Share on Bluesky" [ref=e112] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: https://bsky.app/intent/compose?text=Core%20Web%20Vitals%3A%20Beyond%20the%20Dashboard%20https%3A%2F%2Fericcarlisle.com%2Fblog%2Fcore-web-vitals%2F
                  - img [ref=e113]
            - generic [ref=e115]:
              - heading "On this page" [level=3] [ref=e116]
              - navigation "Table of contents" [ref=e117]:
                - list [ref=e118]:
                  - listitem [ref=e119]:
                    - 'link "LCP: Largest Contentful Paint" [ref=e120] [cursor=pointer]':
                      - /url: "#lcp-largest-contentful-paint"
                  - listitem [ref=e121]:
                    - 'link "CLS: Cumulative Layout Shift" [ref=e122] [cursor=pointer]':
                      - /url: "#cls-cumulative-layout-shift"
                  - listitem [ref=e123]:
                    - 'link "INP: Interaction to Next Frame" [ref=e124] [cursor=pointer]':
                      - /url: "#inp-interaction-to-next-frame"

    - text: © 2026 Eric Carlisle. All rights reserved.

        - /url: https://github.com/ecarlisle

        - img [ref=e134]
```

```yaml

            - generic [ref=e40]: Go to Eric Carlisle's Mastodon profile (opens in new tab)
            - img [ref=e41]
  - main [ref=e43]:
    - link "← Back to all posts" [ref=e44] [cursor=pointer]:
      - /url: /blog
    - article [ref=e45]:
      - img "Design Tokens on This Site" [ref=e47]
      - generic [ref=e48]:
        - generic [ref=e49]:
          - generic [ref=e50]:
            - generic [ref=e51]:
              - generic [ref=e52]:
                - time [ref=e53]: Jun 16, 2026
                - generic [ref=e54]: ·3 min read
              - heading "Design Tokens on This Site" [level=1] [ref=e55]
              - separator [ref=e56]
            - paragraph [ref=e57]:
              - text: "Every visual decision on this site flows through CSS custom properties defined in a single file:"
              - code [ref=e58]: src/styles/global.css
              - text: . This article documents those tokens, their relationships, and the rationale behind them.
            - heading "Typography" [level=2] [ref=e59]
            - heading "Font Families" [level=3] [ref=e60]
            - figure [ref=e62]:
              - code [ref=e64]:
                - generic [ref=e66]: "--font-copy: \"Inter Variable\", system-ui, sans-serif;"
                - generic [ref=e68]: "--font-headers: \"Plus Jakarta Sans Variable\", system-ui, sans-serif;"
                - generic [ref=e70]: "--font-mono: \"Fira Code Variable\", ui-monospace, monospace;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e72] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e74]: Inter is the workhorse body face — narrow, tall x-height, excellent at small sizes. Plus Jakarta Sans handles headings with a more geometric, open character. Fira Code provides monospace with ligatures.
            - heading "Type Size Scale" [level=3] [ref=e75]
            - paragraph [ref=e76]:
              - text: Every size is fluid — it scales smoothly between viewport widths using
              - code [ref=e77]: clamp()
              - text: ":"
            - figure [ref=e79]:
              - code [ref=e81]:
                - generic [ref=e83]: "--type-size-body: clamp(1rem, 0.45vw + 0.91rem, 1.25rem); /* 16px → 20px */"
                - generic [ref=e85]: "--type-size-small: clamp(0.8125rem, 0.2vw + 0.77rem, 0.9375rem); /* 13px → 15px */"
                - generic [ref=e87]: "--type-size-h1: clamp(2rem, 3.64vw + 1.27rem, 4rem); /* 32px → 64px */"
                - generic [ref=e89]: "--type-size-h2: clamp(1.625rem, 2.13vw + 1.2rem, 2.8rem); /* 26px → 45px */"
                - generic [ref=e91]: "--type-size-h3: clamp(1.375rem, 1.14vw + 1.15rem, 2rem); /* 22px → 32px */"
                - generic [ref=e93]: "--type-size-h4: clamp(1.125rem, 0.57vw + 1.07rem, 1.5rem); /* 18px → 24px */"
                - generic [ref=e95]: "--type-size-h5: clamp(1rem, 0.28vw + 0.94rem, 1.1875rem); /* 16px → 19px */"
                - generic [ref=e97]: "--type-size-blockquote: 1.1em;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e99] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e101]:
              - text: The scale uses a roughly 1.25 modular ratio (major third). The
              - code [ref=e102]: clamp()
              - text: endpoints are chosen so that body text never drops below 16 px (the minimum for comfortable long-form reading on a 13″ laptop) and never exceeds it by more than 25 %.
            - heading "Line Heights" [level=3] [ref=e103]
            - figure [ref=e105]:
              - code [ref=e107]:
                - generic [ref=e109]: "--line-height-body: 1.65; /* body copy — generous for readability */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e111] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e113]: There is no separate heading line-height token. Each heading level sets its own line-height as a multiple of the vertical rhythm unit (see below).
            - heading "Vertical Rhythm" [level=2] [ref=e114]
            - paragraph [ref=e115]: "The entire vertical spacing system is anchored to one value:"
            - figure [ref=e117]:
              - code [ref=e119]:
                - generic [ref=e121]: "--rhythm: calc(var(--line-height-body) * 1rem); /* ≈ 1.65 rem */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e123] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e125]:
              - code [ref=e126]: "--rhythm"
              - text: equals one line of body text. Every margin, padding, and heading line-height is a clean multiple of this unit, creating a consistent baseline grid.
            - heading "Heading Sizes and Spacing" [level=3] [ref=e127]
            - table [ref=e128]:
              - rowgroup [ref=e129]:
                - row "Heading Font size (fluid) Line-height (× rhythm) Top margin Bottom margin" [ref=e130]:
                  - columnheader "Heading" [ref=e131]
                  - columnheader "Font size (fluid)" [ref=e132]
                  - columnheader "Line-height (× rhythm)" [ref=e133]
                  - columnheader "Top margin" [ref=e134]
                  - columnheader "Bottom margin" [ref=e135]
              - rowgroup [ref=e136]:
                - row "h1 32 → 64 px 3× (≈ 79 px) 0 1× (≈ 26 px)" [ref=e137]:
                  - cell "h1" [ref=e138]
                  - cell "32 → 64 px" [ref=e139]
                  - cell "3× (≈ 79 px)" [ref=e140]
                  - cell "0" [ref=e141]
                  - cell "1× (≈ 26 px)" [ref=e142]
                - row "h2 26 → 45 px 2× (≈ 53 px) 2× 1×" [ref=e143]:
                  - cell "h2" [ref=e144]
                  - cell "26 → 45 px" [ref=e145]
                  - cell "2× (≈ 53 px)" [ref=e146]
                  - cell "2×" [ref=e147]
                  - cell "1×" [ref=e148]
                - row "h3 22 → 32 px 2× (≈ 53 px) 1.5× 0.5×" [ref=e149]:
                  - cell "h3" [ref=e150]
                  - cell "22 → 32 px" [ref=e151]
                  - cell "2× (≈ 53 px)" [ref=e152]
                  - cell "1.5×" [ref=e153]
                  - cell "0.5×" [ref=e154]
                - row "h4 18 → 24 px 1.5× (≈ 40 px) 1.5× 0.5×" [ref=e155]:
                  - cell "h4" [ref=e156]
                  - cell "18 → 24 px" [ref=e157]
                  - cell "1.5× (≈ 40 px)" [ref=e158]
                  - cell "1.5×" [ref=e159]
                  - cell "0.5×" [ref=e160]
                - row "h5 16 → 19 px 1.25× (≈ 33 px) 1× 0.5×" [ref=e161]:
                  - cell "h5" [ref=e162]
                  - cell "16 → 19 px" [ref=e163]
                  - cell "1.25× (≈ 33 px)" [ref=e164]
                  - cell "1×" [ref=e165]
                  - cell "0.5×" [ref=e166]
            - paragraph [ref=e167]: The bottom margin of a heading is always smaller than its top margin, so each heading visually attaches to the content below it rather than floating between sections.
            - heading "Spacing Scale" [level=2] [ref=e168]
            - paragraph [ref=e169]:
              - text: The spacing tokens provide the building blocks for layout, padding, and gaps. Every value is a multiple of
              - code [ref=e170]: "--rhythm"
              - text: ":"
            - figure [ref=e172]:
              - code [ref=e174]:
                - generic [ref=e176]: "--space-xs: calc(var(--rhythm) * 0.5); /* 0.5× ≈ 13 px */"
                - generic [ref=e178]: "--space-sm: calc(var(--rhythm) * 0.75); /* 0.75× ≈ 20 px */"
                - generic [ref=e180]: "--space-md: calc(var(--rhythm) * 1); /* 1× ≈ 26 px */"
                - generic [ref=e182]: "--space-lg: calc(var(--rhythm) * 1.5); /* 1.5× ≈ 40 px */"
                - generic [ref=e184]: "--space-xl: calc(var(--rhythm) * 2); /* 2× ≈ 53 px */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e186] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e188]: "These are further aliased by purpose so that changing one token adjusts spacing everywhere it is used:"
            - figure [ref=e190]:
              - code [ref=e192]:
                - generic [ref=e194]: "--space-inline: var(--space-xs); /* tight inline gaps (tag spacing, icon gaps) */"
                - generic [ref=e196]: "--space-inline-strong: var(--space-sm); /* stronger inline separation (card children) */"
                - generic [ref=e198]: "--space-component: var(--space-md); /* default component padding */"
                - generic [ref=e200]: "--space-component-lg: var(--space-lg); /* large component padding */"
                - generic [ref=e202]: "--space-section: var(--space-lg); /* section-level margins (headings, blockquotes) */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e204] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Color Palette" [level=2] [ref=e206]
            - paragraph [ref=e207]: The colors use the OKLCH color space for perceptual uniformity. The default theme is a warm dark mode with an amber/coral editorial palette.
            - paragraph [ref=e208]:
              - text: "Both override mechanisms are supported: the"
              - code [ref=e209]: "[data-theme=\"light\"]"
              - text: attribute selector for the JS theme toggle, and
              - code [ref=e210]: "@media (prefers-color-scheme: light)"
              - text: for system-level preference. They hold identical values, so the light appearance is the same regardless of which trigger fires.
            - heading "Dark Theme (default)" [level=3] [ref=e211]
            - figure [ref=e213]:
              - code [ref=e215]:
                - generic [ref=e217]: "--bg-main: oklch(0.12 0.015 280); /* deep navy-black background */"
                - generic [ref=e219]: "--bg-surface: oklch(0.16 0.02 280); /* card / surface background */"
                - generic [ref=e221]: "--bg-surface-elevated: oklch(0.2 0.025 280); /* elevated surface (hovered cards) */"
                - generic [ref=e225]: "--text-primary: oklch(0.95 0.008 280); /* main body text — 15.05:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e227]: "--text-secondary: oklch(0.77 0.015 280); /* secondary text — 4.50:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e229]: "--text-muted: oklch(0.72 0.02 280); /* muted / meta text — 3.28:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e233]: "--brand-primary: oklch(0.64 0.16 75); /* Primary Amber */"
                - generic [ref=e235]: "--brand-accent: oklch(0.58 0.18 35); /* Accent Coral */"
                - generic [ref=e237]: "--brand-highlight: oklch(0.81 0.15 110); /* Highlight Chartreuse */"
                - generic [ref=e239]: "--neutral-anchor: oklch(0.53 0.08 230); /* Anchor Slate */"
                - generic [ref=e241]: "--accent-cold: oklch(0.69 0.15 250); /* Interaction Cerulean */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e243] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Light Theme Override" [level=3] [ref=e245]
            - paragraph [ref=e246]: "The light theme is triggered by either the attribute selector or the system preference media query, and overrides only the color tokens. Typography and spacing stay the same:"
            - figure [ref=e248]:
              - code [ref=e250]:
                - generic [ref=e252]: "[data-theme=\"light\"] {"
                - generic [ref=e254]: "--bg-main: oklch(0.985 0.003 280); /* near-white background */"
                - generic [ref=e256]: "--bg-surface: oklch(1 0 0); /* pure white cards */"
                - generic [ref=e258]: "--bg-surface-elevated: oklch(0.92 0.008 280);"
                - generic [ref=e262]: "--text-primary: oklch(0.13 0.015 280);"
                - generic [ref=e264]: "--text-secondary: oklch(0.25 0.02 280);"
                - generic [ref=e266]: "--text-muted: oklch(0.38 0.025 280);"
                - generic [ref=e270]: "--brand-primary: oklch(0.55 0.15 75); /* Rich Amber */"
                - generic [ref=e272]: "--brand-accent: oklch(0.48 0.16 35); /* Deep Coral */"
                - generic [ref=e274]: "--brand-highlight: oklch(0.45 0.14 110); /* High-Contrast Chartreuse */"
                - generic [ref=e276]: "--neutral-anchor: oklch(0.35 0.06 230); /* Dark Slate Anchor */"
                - generic [ref=e278]: "--accent-cold: oklch(0.52 0.14 250); /* Deep Interactive Cerulean */"
                - generic [ref=e280]: "}"
                - generic [ref=e284]: "@media (prefers-color-scheme: light) {"
                - generic [ref=e286]: ":root { /* identical values */ }"
                - generic [ref=e288]: "}"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e290] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e292]: Using OKLCH ensures that the light and dark themes stay perceptually consistent — the same hue angles produce recognizably the same color families across themes, differing only in lightness. Every text token on both themes exceeds WCAG AA contrast against its background.
            - heading "Border Radii" [level=2] [ref=e293]
            - figure [ref=e295]:
              - code [ref=e297]:
                - generic [ref=e299]: "--radius-sm: 4px;"
                - generic [ref=e301]: "--radius-md: 8px;"
                - generic [ref=e303]: "--radius-lg: 12px;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e305] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e307]:
              - text: A tight radius system. Cards use
              - code [ref=e308]: "--radius-lg"
              - text: ", buttons and inputs use"
              - code [ref=e309]: "--radius-md"
              - text: ", code blocks use"
              - code [ref=e310]: "--radius-sm"
              - text: .
            - heading "Motion" [level=2] [ref=e311]
            - figure [ref=e313]:
              - code [ref=e315]:
                - generic [ref=e317]: "--transition-fast: 0.15s ease-out;"
                - generic [ref=e319]: "--transition-normal: 0.25s ease-out;"
                - generic [ref=e321]: "--transition-slow: 0.4s ease-out;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e323] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e325]:
              - text: All transitions use
              - code [ref=e326]: ease-out
              - text: — fast in, slow out — which feels responsive because the motion starts immediately and coasts to rest. The
              - code [ref=e327]: "prefers-reduced-motion: reduce"
              - text: media query disables all transitions and animations.
            - heading "Structural Tokens" [level=2] [ref=e328]
            - figure [ref=e330]:
              - code [ref=e332]:
                - generic [ref=e334]: "--size-border-pixel: 2px;"
                - generic [ref=e336]: "--size-border-thick: 4px;"
                - generic [ref=e338]: "--size-touch-target-min: 44px;"
                - generic [ref=e342]: "--layout-max-width: 68ch; /* comfortable reading measure */"
                - generic [ref=e344]: "--layout-max-width-wide: 75rem; /* full page content area */"
                - generic [ref=e346]: "--bp-mobile: 720px; /* mobile breakpoint */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e348] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e350]:
              - text: The 68-character line length for body text follows the established range of 45–75 characters per line. The touch target minimum of 44px matches
              - link "WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 2.5.8" [ref=e351] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/#target-size
              - text: .
          - generic [ref=e353]:
            - button "css 10" [ref=e354] [cursor=pointer]:
              - text: css
              - superscript [ref=e355]: "10"
            - button "design-systems 4" [ref=e356] [cursor=pointer]:
              - text: design-systems
              - superscript [ref=e357]: "4"
          - navigation "Pagination" [ref=e358]:
            - link "← Previous Markdown Style Guide" [ref=e359] [cursor=pointer]:
              - /url: /blog/markdown-style-guide/
              - generic [ref=e360]: ← Previous

            - 'link "Next → Choosing a Color Palette: From Cold Blue to Warm Amber" [ref=e361] [cursor=pointer]':

              - generic [ref=e362]: Next →
              - text: "Choosing a Color Palette: From Cold Blue to Warm Amber"
        - complementary [ref=e363]:
          - generic [ref=e364]:
            - generic [ref=e366]:
              - generic [ref=e367]: Share
```

```yaml
- generic [active] [ref=e1]:
```
