          - link "Go to Eric Carlisle's Mastodon profile (opens in new tab)" [ref=e39] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://fosstodon.org/@ericcarlisle
            - generic [ref=e40]: Go to Eric Carlisle's Mastodon profile (opens in new tab)
            - img [ref=e41]
  - main [ref=e43]:
    - link "← Back to all posts" [ref=e44] [cursor=pointer]:
      - /url: /blog
    - article [ref=e45]:
      - img "Design Tokens on This Site" [ref=e47]
      - generic [ref=e48]:
        - generic [ref=e49]:
          - generic [ref=e50]:
            - generic [ref=e51]:
              - generic [ref=e52]:
                - time [ref=e53]: Jun 16, 2026
                - generic [ref=e54]: ·3 min read
              - heading "Design Tokens on This Site" [level=1] [ref=e55]
              - separator [ref=e56]
            - paragraph [ref=e57]:
              - text: "Every visual decision on this site flows through CSS custom properties defined in a single file:"
              - code [ref=e58]: src/styles/global.css
              - text: . This article documents those tokens, their relationships, and the rationale behind them.
            - heading "Typography" [level=2] [ref=e59]
            - heading "Font Families" [level=3] [ref=e60]
            - figure [ref=e62]:
              - code [ref=e64]:
                - generic [ref=e66]: "--font-copy: \"Inter Variable\", system-ui, sans-serif;"
                - generic [ref=e68]: "--font-headers: \"Plus Jakarta Sans Variable\", system-ui, sans-serif;"
                - generic [ref=e70]: "--font-mono: \"Fira Code Variable\", ui-monospace, monospace;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e72] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e74]: Inter is the workhorse body face — narrow, tall x-height, excellent at small sizes. Plus Jakarta Sans handles headings with a more geometric, open character. Fira Code provides monospace with ligatures.
            - heading "Type Size Scale" [level=3] [ref=e75]
            - paragraph [ref=e76]:
              - text: Every size is fluid — it scales smoothly between viewport widths using
              - code [ref=e77]: clamp()
              - text: ":"
            - figure [ref=e79]:
              - code [ref=e81]:
                - generic [ref=e83]: "--type-size-body: clamp(1rem, 0.45vw + 0.91rem, 1.25rem); /* 16px → 20px */"
                - generic [ref=e85]: "--type-size-small: clamp(0.8125rem, 0.2vw + 0.77rem, 0.9375rem); /* 13px → 15px */"
                - generic [ref=e87]: "--type-size-h1: clamp(2rem, 3.64vw + 1.27rem, 4rem); /* 32px → 64px */"
                - generic [ref=e89]: "--type-size-h2: clamp(1.625rem, 2.13vw + 1.2rem, 2.8rem); /* 26px → 45px */"
                - generic [ref=e91]: "--type-size-h3: clamp(1.375rem, 1.14vw + 1.15rem, 2rem); /* 22px → 32px */"
                - generic [ref=e93]: "--type-size-h4: clamp(1.125rem, 0.57vw + 1.07rem, 1.5rem); /* 18px → 24px */"
                - generic [ref=e95]: "--type-size-h5: clamp(1rem, 0.28vw + 0.94rem, 1.1875rem); /* 16px → 19px */"
                - generic [ref=e97]: "--type-size-blockquote: 1.1em;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e99] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e101]:
              - text: The scale uses a roughly 1.25 modular ratio (major third). The
              - code [ref=e102]: clamp()
              - text: endpoints are chosen so that body text never drops below 16 px (the minimum for comfortable long-form reading on a 13″ laptop) and never exceeds it by more than 25 %.
            - heading "Line Heights" [level=3] [ref=e103]
            - figure [ref=e105]:
              - code [ref=e107]:
                - generic [ref=e109]: "--line-height-body: 1.65; /* body copy — generous for readability */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e111] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e113]: There is no separate heading line-height token. Each heading level sets its own line-height as a multiple of the vertical rhythm unit (see below).
            - heading "Vertical Rhythm" [level=2] [ref=e114]
            - paragraph [ref=e115]: "The entire vertical spacing system is anchored to one value:"
            - figure [ref=e117]:
              - code [ref=e119]:
                - generic [ref=e121]: "--rhythm: calc(var(--line-height-body) * 1rem); /* ≈ 1.65 rem */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e123] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e125]:
              - code [ref=e126]: "--rhythm"
              - text: equals one line of body text. Every margin, padding, and heading line-height is a clean multiple of this unit, creating a consistent baseline grid.
            - heading "Heading Sizes and Spacing" [level=3] [ref=e127]
            - table [ref=e128]:
              - rowgroup [ref=e129]:
                - row "Heading Font size (fluid) Line-height (× rhythm) Top margin Bottom margin" [ref=e130]:
                  - columnheader "Heading" [ref=e131]
                  - columnheader "Font size (fluid)" [ref=e132]
                  - columnheader "Line-height (× rhythm)" [ref=e133]
                  - columnheader "Top margin" [ref=e134]
                  - columnheader "Bottom margin" [ref=e135]
              - rowgroup [ref=e136]:
                - row "h1 32 → 64 px 3× (≈ 79 px) 0 1× (≈ 26 px)" [ref=e137]:
                  - cell "h1" [ref=e138]
                  - cell "32 → 64 px" [ref=e139]
                  - cell "3× (≈ 79 px)" [ref=e140]
                  - cell "0" [ref=e141]
                  - cell "1× (≈ 26 px)" [ref=e142]
                - row "h2 26 → 45 px 2× (≈ 53 px) 2× 1×" [ref=e143]:
                  - cell "h2" [ref=e144]
                  - cell "26 → 45 px" [ref=e145]
                  - cell "2× (≈ 53 px)" [ref=e146]
                  - cell "2×" [ref=e147]
                  - cell "1×" [ref=e148]
                - row "h3 22 → 32 px 2× (≈ 53 px) 1.5× 0.5×" [ref=e149]:
                  - cell "h3" [ref=e150]
                  - cell "22 → 32 px" [ref=e151]
                  - cell "2× (≈ 53 px)" [ref=e152]
                  - cell "1.5×" [ref=e153]
                  - cell "0.5×" [ref=e154]
                - row "h4 18 → 24 px 1.5× (≈ 40 px) 1.5× 0.5×" [ref=e155]:
                  - cell "h4" [ref=e156]
                  - cell "18 → 24 px" [ref=e157]
                  - cell "1.5× (≈ 40 px)" [ref=e158]
                  - cell "1.5×" [ref=e159]
                  - cell "0.5×" [ref=e160]
                - row "h5 16 → 19 px 1.25× (≈ 33 px) 1× 0.5×" [ref=e161]:
                  - cell "h5" [ref=e162]
                  - cell "16 → 19 px" [ref=e163]
                  - cell "1.25× (≈ 33 px)" [ref=e164]
                  - cell "1×" [ref=e165]
                  - cell "0.5×" [ref=e166]
            - paragraph [ref=e167]: The bottom margin of a heading is always smaller than its top margin, so each heading visually attaches to the content below it rather than floating between sections.
            - heading "Spacing Scale" [level=2] [ref=e168]
            - paragraph [ref=e169]:
              - text: The spacing tokens provide the building blocks for layout, padding, and gaps. Every value is a multiple of
              - code [ref=e170]: "--rhythm"
              - text: ":"
            - figure [ref=e172]:
              - code [ref=e174]:
                - generic [ref=e176]: "--space-xs: calc(var(--rhythm) * 0.5); /* 0.5× ≈ 13 px */"
                - generic [ref=e178]: "--space-sm: calc(var(--rhythm) * 0.75); /* 0.75× ≈ 20 px */"
                - generic [ref=e180]: "--space-md: calc(var(--rhythm) * 1); /* 1× ≈ 26 px */"
                - generic [ref=e182]: "--space-lg: calc(var(--rhythm) * 1.5); /* 1.5× ≈ 40 px */"
                - generic [ref=e184]: "--space-xl: calc(var(--rhythm) * 2); /* 2× ≈ 53 px */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e186] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e188]: "These are further aliased by purpose so that changing one token adjusts spacing everywhere it is used:"
            - figure [ref=e190]:
              - code [ref=e192]:
                - generic [ref=e194]: "--space-inline: var(--space-xs); /* tight inline gaps (tag spacing, icon gaps) */"
                - generic [ref=e196]: "--space-inline-strong: var(--space-sm); /* stronger inline separation (card children) */"
                - generic [ref=e198]: "--space-component: var(--space-md); /* default component padding */"
                - generic [ref=e200]: "--space-component-lg: var(--space-lg); /* large component padding */"
                - generic [ref=e202]: "--space-section: var(--space-lg); /* section-level margins (headings, blockquotes) */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e204] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Color Palette" [level=2] [ref=e206]
            - paragraph [ref=e207]: The colors use the OKLCH color space for perceptual uniformity. The default theme is a warm dark mode with an amber/coral editorial palette.
            - paragraph [ref=e208]:
              - text: "Both override mechanisms are supported: the"
              - code [ref=e209]: "[data-theme=\"light\"]"
              - text: attribute selector for the JS theme toggle, and
              - code [ref=e210]: "@media (prefers-color-scheme: light)"
              - text: for system-level preference. They hold identical values, so the light appearance is the same regardless of which trigger fires.
            - heading "Dark Theme (default)" [level=3] [ref=e211]
            - figure [ref=e213]:
              - code [ref=e215]:
                - generic [ref=e217]: "--bg-main: oklch(0.12 0.015 280); /* deep navy-black background */"
                - generic [ref=e219]: "--bg-surface: oklch(0.16 0.02 280); /* card / surface background */"
                - generic [ref=e221]: "--bg-surface-elevated: oklch(0.2 0.025 280); /* elevated surface (hovered cards) */"
                - generic [ref=e225]: "--text-primary: oklch(0.95 0.008 280); /* main body text — 15.05:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e227]: "--text-secondary: oklch(0.77 0.015 280); /* secondary text — 4.50:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e229]: "--text-muted: oklch(0.72 0.02 280); /* muted / meta text — 3.28:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e233]: "--brand-primary: oklch(0.64 0.16 75); /* Primary Amber */"
                - generic [ref=e235]: "--brand-accent: oklch(0.58 0.18 35); /* Accent Coral */"
                - generic [ref=e237]: "--brand-highlight: oklch(0.81 0.15 110); /* Highlight Chartreuse */"
                - generic [ref=e239]: "--neutral-anchor: oklch(0.53 0.08 230); /* Anchor Slate */"
                - generic [ref=e241]: "--accent-cold: oklch(0.69 0.15 250); /* Interaction Cerulean */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e243] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Light Theme Override" [level=3] [ref=e245]
            - paragraph [ref=e246]: "The light theme is triggered by either the attribute selector or the system preference media query, and overrides only the color tokens. Typography and spacing stay the same:"
            - figure [ref=e248]:
              - code [ref=e250]:
                - generic [ref=e252]: "[data-theme=\"light\"] {"
                - generic [ref=e254]: "--bg-main: oklch(0.985 0.003 280); /* near-white background */"
                - generic [ref=e256]: "--bg-surface: oklch(1 0 0); /* pure white cards */"
                - generic [ref=e258]: "--bg-surface-elevated: oklch(0.92 0.008 280);"
                - generic [ref=e262]: "--text-primary: oklch(0.13 0.015 280);"
                - generic [ref=e264]: "--text-secondary: oklch(0.25 0.02 280);"
                - generic [ref=e266]: "--text-muted: oklch(0.38 0.025 280);"
                - generic [ref=e270]: "--brand-primary: oklch(0.55 0.15 75); /* Rich Amber */"
                - generic [ref=e272]: "--brand-accent: oklch(0.48 0.16 35); /* Deep Coral */"
                - generic [ref=e274]: "--brand-highlight: oklch(0.45 0.14 110); /* High-Contrast Chartreuse */"
                - generic [ref=e276]: "--neutral-anchor: oklch(0.35 0.06 230); /* Dark Slate Anchor */"
                - generic [ref=e278]: "--accent-cold: oklch(0.52 0.14 250); /* Deep Interactive Cerulean */"
                - generic [ref=e280]: "}"
                - generic [ref=e284]: "@media (prefers-color-scheme: light) {"
                - generic [ref=e286]: ":root { /* identical values */ }"
                - generic [ref=e288]: "}"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e290] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e292]: Using OKLCH ensures that the light and dark themes stay perceptually consistent — the same hue angles produce recognizably the same color families across themes, differing only in lightness. Every text token on both themes exceeds WCAG AA contrast against its background.
            - heading "Border Radii" [level=2] [ref=e293]
            - figure [ref=e295]:
              - code [ref=e297]:
                - generic [ref=e299]: "--radius-sm: 4px;"
                - generic [ref=e301]: "--radius-md: 8px;"
                - generic [ref=e303]: "--radius-lg: 12px;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e305] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e307]:
              - text: A tight radius system. Cards use
              - code [ref=e308]: "--radius-lg"
              - text: ", buttons and inputs use"
              - code [ref=e309]: "--radius-md"
              - text: ", code blocks use"
              - code [ref=e310]: "--radius-sm"
              - text: .
            - heading "Motion" [level=2] [ref=e311]
            - figure [ref=e313]:
              - code [ref=e315]:
                - generic [ref=e317]: "--transition-fast: 0.15s ease-out;"
                - generic [ref=e319]: "--transition-normal: 0.25s ease-out;"
                - generic [ref=e321]: "--transition-slow: 0.4s ease-out;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e323] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e325]:
              - text: All transitions use
              - code [ref=e326]: ease-out
              - text: — fast in, slow out — which feels responsive because the motion starts immediately and coasts to rest. The
              - code [ref=e327]: "prefers-reduced-motion: reduce"
              - text: media query disables all transitions and animations.
            - heading "Structural Tokens" [level=2] [ref=e328]
            - figure [ref=e330]:
              - code [ref=e332]:
                - generic [ref=e334]: "--size-border-pixel: 2px;"
                - generic [ref=e336]: "--size-border-thick: 4px;"
                - generic [ref=e338]: "--size-touch-target-min: 44px;"
                - generic [ref=e342]: "--layout-max-width: 68ch; /* comfortable reading measure */"
                - generic [ref=e344]: "--layout-max-width-wide: 75rem; /* full page content area */"
                - generic [ref=e346]: "--bp-mobile: 720px; /* mobile breakpoint */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e348] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e350]:
              - text: The 68-character line length for body text follows the established range of 45–75 characters per line. The touch target minimum of 44px matches
              - link "WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 2.5.8" [ref=e351] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/#target-size
              - text: .
          - generic [ref=e353]:
            - button "css 10" [ref=e354] [cursor=pointer]:
              - text: css
              - superscript [ref=e355]: "10"
            - button "design-systems 4" [ref=e356] [cursor=pointer]:

  - progressbar "Reading progress"
  - link "Skip to main content" [ref=e2] [cursor=pointer]:

        - generic [ref=e8]:

            - /url: https://github.com/ecarlisle

            - img [ref=e29]
          - link "Go to Eric Carlisle's LinkedIn profile (opens in new tab)" [ref=e31] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://www.linkedin.com/in/ericcarlisle/
            - generic [ref=e32]: Go to Eric Carlisle's LinkedIn profile (opens in new tab)
            - img [ref=e33]
          - link "Go to Eric Carlisle's Bluesky profile (opens in new tab)" [ref=e35] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://bsky.app/profile/ericcarlisle.bsky.social
            - generic [ref=e36]: Go to Eric Carlisle's Bluesky profile (opens in new tab)
            - img [ref=e37]
          - link "Go to Eric Carlisle's Mastodon profile (opens in new tab)" [ref=e39] [cursor=pointer]:
            - /url: https://fosstodon.org/@ericcarlisle
            - generic [ref=e40]: Go to Eric Carlisle's Mastodon profile (opens in new tab)
            - img [ref=e41]
  - main [ref=e43]:
    - link "← Back to all posts" [ref=e44] [cursor=pointer]:
      - /url: /blog
    - article [ref=e45]:
      - img "Design Tokens on This Site" [ref=e47]
      - generic [ref=e48]:
        - generic [ref=e49]:
          - generic [ref=e50]:
            - generic [ref=e51]:
              - generic [ref=e52]:
                - time [ref=e53]: Jun 16, 2026
                - generic [ref=e54]: ·3 min read
              - heading "Design Tokens on This Site" [level=1] [ref=e55]
              - separator [ref=e56]
            - paragraph [ref=e57]:
              - text: "Every visual decision on this site flows through CSS custom properties defined in a single file:"
              - code [ref=e58]: src/styles/global.css
              - text: . This article documents those tokens, their relationships, and the rationale behind them.
            - heading "Typography" [level=2] [ref=e59]
            - heading "Font Families" [level=3] [ref=e60]
            - figure [ref=e62]:
              - code [ref=e64]:
                - generic [ref=e66]: "--font-copy: \"Inter Variable\", system-ui, sans-serif;"
                - generic [ref=e68]: "--font-headers: \"Plus Jakarta Sans Variable\", system-ui, sans-serif;"
                - generic [ref=e70]: "--font-mono: \"Fira Code Variable\", ui-monospace, monospace;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e72] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e74]: Inter is the workhorse body face — narrow, tall x-height, excellent at small sizes. Plus Jakarta Sans handles headings with a more geometric, open character. Fira Code provides monospace with ligatures.
            - heading "Type Size Scale" [level=3] [ref=e75]
            - paragraph [ref=e76]:
              - text: Every size is fluid — it scales smoothly between viewport widths using
              - code [ref=e77]: clamp()
              - text: ":"
            - figure [ref=e79]:
              - code [ref=e81]:
                - generic [ref=e83]: "--type-size-body: clamp(1rem, 0.45vw + 0.91rem, 1.25rem); /* 16px → 20px */"
                - generic [ref=e85]: "--type-size-small: clamp(0.8125rem, 0.2vw + 0.77rem, 0.9375rem); /* 13px → 15px */"
                - generic [ref=e87]: "--type-size-h1: clamp(2rem, 3.64vw + 1.27rem, 4rem); /* 32px → 64px */"
                - generic [ref=e89]: "--type-size-h2: clamp(1.625rem, 2.13vw + 1.2rem, 2.8rem); /* 26px → 45px */"
                - generic [ref=e91]: "--type-size-h3: clamp(1.375rem, 1.14vw + 1.15rem, 2rem); /* 22px → 32px */"
                - generic [ref=e93]: "--type-size-h4: clamp(1.125rem, 0.57vw + 1.07rem, 1.5rem); /* 18px → 24px */"
                - generic [ref=e95]: "--type-size-h5: clamp(1rem, 0.28vw + 0.94rem, 1.1875rem); /* 16px → 19px */"
                - generic [ref=e97]: "--type-size-blockquote: 1.1em;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e99] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e101]:
              - text: The scale uses a roughly 1.25 modular ratio (major third). The
              - code [ref=e102]: clamp()
              - text: endpoints are chosen so that body text never drops below 16 px (the minimum for comfortable long-form reading on a 13″ laptop) and never exceeds it by more than 25 %.
            - heading "Line Heights" [level=3] [ref=e103]
            - figure [ref=e105]:
              - code [ref=e107]:
                - generic [ref=e109]: "--line-height-body: 1.65; /* body copy — generous for readability */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e111] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e113]: There is no separate heading line-height token. Each heading level sets its own line-height as a multiple of the vertical rhythm unit (see below).
            - heading "Vertical Rhythm" [level=2] [ref=e114]
            - paragraph [ref=e115]: "The entire vertical spacing system is anchored to one value:"
            - figure [ref=e117]:
              - code [ref=e119]:
                - generic [ref=e121]: "--rhythm: calc(var(--line-height-body) * 1rem); /* ≈ 1.65 rem */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e123] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e125]:
              - code [ref=e126]: "--rhythm"
              - text: equals one line of body text. Every margin, padding, and heading line-height is a clean multiple of this unit, creating a consistent baseline grid.
            - heading "Heading Sizes and Spacing" [level=3] [ref=e127]
            - table [ref=e128]:
              - rowgroup [ref=e129]:
                - row "Heading Font size (fluid) Line-height (× rhythm) Top margin Bottom margin" [ref=e130]:
                  - columnheader "Heading" [ref=e131]
                  - columnheader "Font size (fluid)" [ref=e132]
                  - columnheader "Line-height (× rhythm)" [ref=e133]
                  - columnheader "Top margin" [ref=e134]
                  - columnheader "Bottom margin" [ref=e135]
              - rowgroup [ref=e136]:
                - row "h1 32 → 64 px 3× (≈ 79 px) 0 1× (≈ 26 px)" [ref=e137]:
                  - cell "h1" [ref=e138]
                  - cell "32 → 64 px" [ref=e139]
                  - cell "3× (≈ 79 px)" [ref=e140]
                  - cell "0" [ref=e141]
                  - cell "1× (≈ 26 px)" [ref=e142]
                - row "h2 26 → 45 px 2× (≈ 53 px) 2× 1×" [ref=e143]:
                  - cell "h2" [ref=e144]
                  - cell "26 → 45 px" [ref=e145]
                  - cell "2× (≈ 53 px)" [ref=e146]
                  - cell "2×" [ref=e147]
                  - cell "1×" [ref=e148]
                - row "h3 22 → 32 px 2× (≈ 53 px) 1.5× 0.5×" [ref=e149]:
                  - cell "h3" [ref=e150]
                  - cell "22 → 32 px" [ref=e151]
                  - cell "2× (≈ 53 px)" [ref=e152]
                  - cell "1.5×" [ref=e153]
                  - cell "0.5×" [ref=e154]
                - row "h4 18 → 24 px 1.5× (≈ 40 px) 1.5× 0.5×" [ref=e155]:
                  - cell "h4" [ref=e156]
                  - cell "18 → 24 px" [ref=e157]
                  - cell "1.5× (≈ 40 px)" [ref=e158]
                  - cell "1.5×" [ref=e159]
                  - cell "0.5×" [ref=e160]
                - row "h5 16 → 19 px 1.25× (≈ 33 px) 1× 0.5×" [ref=e161]:
                  - cell "h5" [ref=e162]
                  - cell "16 → 19 px" [ref=e163]
                  - cell "1.25× (≈ 33 px)" [ref=e164]
                  - cell "1×" [ref=e165]
                  - cell "0.5×" [ref=e166]
            - paragraph [ref=e167]: The bottom margin of a heading is always smaller than its top margin, so each heading visually attaches to the content below it rather than floating between sections.
            - heading "Spacing Scale" [level=2] [ref=e168]
            - paragraph [ref=e169]:
              - text: The spacing tokens provide the building blocks for layout, padding, and gaps. Every value is a multiple of
              - code [ref=e170]: "--rhythm"
              - text: ":"
            - figure [ref=e172]:
              - code [ref=e174]:
                - generic [ref=e176]: "--space-xs: calc(var(--rhythm) * 0.5); /* 0.5× ≈ 13 px */"
                - generic [ref=e178]: "--space-sm: calc(var(--rhythm) * 0.75); /* 0.75× ≈ 20 px */"
                - generic [ref=e180]: "--space-md: calc(var(--rhythm) * 1); /* 1× ≈ 26 px */"
                - generic [ref=e182]: "--space-lg: calc(var(--rhythm) * 1.5); /* 1.5× ≈ 40 px */"
                - generic [ref=e184]: "--space-xl: calc(var(--rhythm) * 2); /* 2× ≈ 53 px */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e186] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e188]: "These are further aliased by purpose so that changing one token adjusts spacing everywhere it is used:"
            - figure [ref=e190]:
              - code [ref=e192]:
                - generic [ref=e194]: "--space-inline: var(--space-xs); /* tight inline gaps (tag spacing, icon gaps) */"
                - generic [ref=e196]: "--space-inline-strong: var(--space-sm); /* stronger inline separation (card children) */"
                - generic [ref=e198]: "--space-component: var(--space-md); /* default component padding */"
                - generic [ref=e200]: "--space-component-lg: var(--space-lg); /* large component padding */"
                - generic [ref=e202]: "--space-section: var(--space-lg); /* section-level margins (headings, blockquotes) */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e204] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Color Palette" [level=2] [ref=e206]
            - paragraph [ref=e207]: The colors use the OKLCH color space for perceptual uniformity. The default theme is a warm dark mode with an amber/coral editorial palette.
            - paragraph [ref=e208]:
              - text: "Both override mechanisms are supported: the"
              - code [ref=e209]: "[data-theme=\"light\"]"
              - text: attribute selector for the JS theme toggle, and
              - code [ref=e210]: "@media (prefers-color-scheme: light)"
              - text: for system-level preference. They hold identical values, so the light appearance is the same regardless of which trigger fires.
            - heading "Dark Theme (default)" [level=3] [ref=e211]
            - figure [ref=e213]:
              - code [ref=e215]:
                - generic [ref=e217]: "--bg-main: oklch(0.12 0.015 280); /* deep navy-black background */"
                - generic [ref=e219]: "--bg-surface: oklch(0.16 0.02 280); /* card / surface background */"
                - generic [ref=e221]: "--bg-surface-elevated: oklch(0.2 0.025 280); /* elevated surface (hovered cards) */"
                - generic [ref=e225]: "--text-primary: oklch(0.95 0.008 280); /* main body text — 15.05:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e227]: "--text-secondary: oklch(0.77 0.015 280); /* secondary text — 4.50:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e229]: "--text-muted: oklch(0.72 0.02 280); /* muted / meta text — 3.28:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e233]: "--brand-primary: oklch(0.64 0.16 75); /* Primary Amber */"
                - generic [ref=e235]: "--brand-accent: oklch(0.58 0.18 35); /* Accent Coral */"
                - generic [ref=e237]: "--brand-highlight: oklch(0.81 0.15 110); /* Highlight Chartreuse */"
                - generic [ref=e239]: "--neutral-anchor: oklch(0.53 0.08 230); /* Anchor Slate */"
                - generic [ref=e241]: "--accent-cold: oklch(0.69 0.15 250); /* Interaction Cerulean */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e243] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Light Theme Override" [level=3] [ref=e245]
            - paragraph [ref=e246]: "The light theme is triggered by either the attribute selector or the system preference media query, and overrides only the color tokens. Typography and spacing stay the same:"
            - figure [ref=e248]:
              - code [ref=e250]:
                - generic [ref=e252]: "[data-theme=\"light\"] {"
                - generic [ref=e254]: "--bg-main: oklch(0.985 0.003 280); /* near-white background */"
                - generic [ref=e256]: "--bg-surface: oklch(1 0 0); /* pure white cards */"
                - generic [ref=e258]: "--bg-surface-elevated: oklch(0.92 0.008 280);"
                - generic [ref=e262]: "--text-primary: oklch(0.13 0.015 280);"
                - generic [ref=e264]: "--text-secondary: oklch(0.25 0.02 280);"
                - generic [ref=e266]: "--text-muted: oklch(0.38 0.025 280);"
                - generic [ref=e270]: "--brand-primary: oklch(0.55 0.15 75); /* Rich Amber */"
                - generic [ref=e272]: "--brand-accent: oklch(0.48 0.16 35); /* Deep Coral */"
                - generic [ref=e274]: "--brand-highlight: oklch(0.45 0.14 110); /* High-Contrast Chartreuse */"
                - generic [ref=e276]: "--neutral-anchor: oklch(0.35 0.06 230); /* Dark Slate Anchor */"
                - generic [ref=e278]: "--accent-cold: oklch(0.52 0.14 250); /* Deep Interactive Cerulean */"
                - generic [ref=e280]: "}"
                - generic [ref=e284]: "@media (prefers-color-scheme: light) {"
                - generic [ref=e286]: ":root { /* identical values */ }"
                - generic [ref=e288]: "}"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e290] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e292]: Using OKLCH ensures that the light and dark themes stay perceptually consistent — the same hue angles produce recognizably the same color families across themes, differing only in lightness. Every text token on both themes exceeds WCAG AA contrast against its background.
            - heading "Border Radii" [level=2] [ref=e293]
            - figure [ref=e295]:
              - code [ref=e297]:
                - generic [ref=e299]: "--radius-sm: 4px;"
                - generic [ref=e301]: "--radius-md: 8px;"
                - generic [ref=e303]: "--radius-lg: 12px;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e305] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e307]:
              - text: A tight radius system. Cards use
              - code [ref=e308]: "--radius-lg"
              - text: ", buttons and inputs use"
              - code [ref=e309]: "--radius-md"
              - text: ", code blocks use"
              - code [ref=e310]: "--radius-sm"
              - text: .
            - heading "Motion" [level=2] [ref=e311]
            - figure [ref=e313]:
              - code [ref=e315]:
                - generic [ref=e317]: "--transition-fast: 0.15s ease-out;"
                - generic [ref=e319]: "--transition-normal: 0.25s ease-out;"
                - generic [ref=e321]: "--transition-slow: 0.4s ease-out;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e323] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e325]:
              - text: All transitions use
              - code [ref=e326]: ease-out
              - text: — fast in, slow out — which feels responsive because the motion starts immediately and coasts to rest. The
              - code [ref=e327]: "prefers-reduced-motion: reduce"
              - text: media query disables all transitions and animations.
            - heading "Structural Tokens" [level=2] [ref=e328]
            - figure [ref=e330]:
              - code [ref=e332]:
                - generic [ref=e334]: "--size-border-pixel: 2px;"
                - generic [ref=e336]: "--size-border-thick: 4px;"
                - generic [ref=e338]: "--size-touch-target-min: 44px;"
                - generic [ref=e342]: "--layout-max-width: 68ch; /* comfortable reading measure */"
                - generic [ref=e344]: "--layout-max-width-wide: 75rem; /* full page content area */"
                - generic [ref=e346]: "--bp-mobile: 720px; /* mobile breakpoint */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e348] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e350]:
              - text: The 68-character line length for body text follows the established range of 45–75 characters per line. The touch target minimum of 44px matches
              - link "WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 2.5.8" [ref=e351] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/#target-size
              - text: .
          - generic [ref=e353]:
            - button "css 10" [ref=e354] [cursor=pointer]:
              - text: css
              - superscript [ref=e355]: "10"
            - button "design-systems 4" [ref=e356] [cursor=pointer]:
              - text: design-systems
              - superscript [ref=e357]: "4"
          - navigation "Pagination" [ref=e358]:

  - main [ref=e43]:
    - link "← Back to all posts" [ref=e44] [cursor=pointer]:
      - /url: /blog
    - article [ref=e45]:
      - img "Design Tokens on This Site" [ref=e47]
      - generic [ref=e48]:
        - generic [ref=e49]:
          - generic [ref=e50]:
            - generic [ref=e51]:
              - generic [ref=e52]:
                - time [ref=e53]: Jun 16, 2026
                - generic [ref=e54]: ·3 min read
              - heading "Design Tokens on This Site" [level=1] [ref=e55]
              - separator [ref=e56]
            - paragraph [ref=e57]:
              - text: "Every visual decision on this site flows through CSS custom properties defined in a single file:"
              - code [ref=e58]: src/styles/global.css
              - text: . This article documents those tokens, their relationships, and the rationale behind them.
            - heading "Typography" [level=2] [ref=e59]
            - heading "Font Families" [level=3] [ref=e60]
            - figure [ref=e62]:
              - code [ref=e64]:
                - generic [ref=e66]: "--font-copy: \"Inter Variable\", system-ui, sans-serif;"
                - generic [ref=e68]: "--font-headers: \"Plus Jakarta Sans Variable\", system-ui, sans-serif;"
                - generic [ref=e70]: "--font-mono: \"Fira Code Variable\", ui-monospace, monospace;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e72] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e74]: Inter is the workhorse body face — narrow, tall x-height, excellent at small sizes. Plus Jakarta Sans handles headings with a more geometric, open character. Fira Code provides monospace with ligatures.
            - heading "Type Size Scale" [level=3] [ref=e75]
            - paragraph [ref=e76]:
              - text: Every size is fluid — it scales smoothly between viewport widths using
              - code [ref=e77]: clamp()
              - text: ":"
            - figure [ref=e79]:
              - code [ref=e81]:
                - generic [ref=e83]: "--type-size-body: clamp(1rem, 0.45vw + 0.91rem, 1.25rem); /* 16px → 20px */"
                - generic [ref=e85]: "--type-size-small: clamp(0.8125rem, 0.2vw + 0.77rem, 0.9375rem); /* 13px → 15px */"
                - generic [ref=e87]: "--type-size-h1: clamp(2rem, 3.64vw + 1.27rem, 4rem); /* 32px → 64px */"
                - generic [ref=e89]: "--type-size-h2: clamp(1.625rem, 2.13vw + 1.2rem, 2.8rem); /* 26px → 45px */"
                - generic [ref=e91]: "--type-size-h3: clamp(1.375rem, 1.14vw + 1.15rem, 2rem); /* 22px → 32px */"
                - generic [ref=e93]: "--type-size-h4: clamp(1.125rem, 0.57vw + 1.07rem, 1.5rem); /* 18px → 24px */"
                - generic [ref=e95]: "--type-size-h5: clamp(1rem, 0.28vw + 0.94rem, 1.1875rem); /* 16px → 19px */"
                - generic [ref=e97]: "--type-size-blockquote: 1.1em;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e99] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e101]:
              - text: The scale uses a roughly 1.25 modular ratio (major third). The
              - code [ref=e102]: clamp()
              - text: endpoints are chosen so that body text never drops below 16 px (the minimum for comfortable long-form reading on a 13″ laptop) and never exceeds it by more than 25 %.
            - heading "Line Heights" [level=3] [ref=e103]
            - figure [ref=e105]:
              - code [ref=e107]:
                - generic [ref=e109]: "--line-height-body: 1.65; /* body copy — generous for readability */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e111] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e113]: There is no separate heading line-height token. Each heading level sets its own line-height as a multiple of the vertical rhythm unit (see below).
            - heading "Vertical Rhythm" [level=2] [ref=e114]
            - paragraph [ref=e115]: "The entire vertical spacing system is anchored to one value:"
            - figure [ref=e117]:
              - code [ref=e119]:
                - generic [ref=e121]: "--rhythm: calc(var(--line-height-body) * 1rem); /* ≈ 1.65 rem */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e123] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e125]:
              - code [ref=e126]: "--rhythm"
              - text: equals one line of body text. Every margin, padding, and heading line-height is a clean multiple of this unit, creating a consistent baseline grid.
            - heading "Heading Sizes and Spacing" [level=3] [ref=e127]

            - paragraph [ref=e169]:

                - generic [ref=e196]: "--space-inline-strong: var(--space-sm); /* stronger inline separation (card children) */"
                - generic [ref=e198]: "--space-component: var(--space-md); /* default component padding */"
                - generic [ref=e200]: "--space-component-lg: var(--space-lg); /* large component padding */"
                - generic [ref=e202]: "--space-section: var(--space-lg); /* section-level margins (headings, blockquotes) */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e204] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Color Palette" [level=2] [ref=e206]
            - paragraph [ref=e207]: The colors use the OKLCH color space for perceptual uniformity. The default theme is a warm dark mode with an amber/coral editorial palette.
            - paragraph [ref=e208]:
              - text: "Both override mechanisms are supported: the"
              - code [ref=e209]: "[data-theme=\"light\"]"
              - text: attribute selector for the JS theme toggle, and
              - code [ref=e210]: "@media (prefers-color-scheme: light)"
              - text: for system-level preference. They hold identical values, so the light appearance is the same regardless of which trigger fires.
            - heading "Dark Theme (default)" [level=3] [ref=e211]
            - figure [ref=e213]:
              - code [ref=e215]:
                - generic [ref=e217]: "--bg-main: oklch(0.12 0.015 280); /* deep navy-black background */"
                - generic [ref=e219]: "--bg-surface: oklch(0.16 0.02 280); /* card / surface background */"
                - generic [ref=e221]: "--bg-surface-elevated: oklch(0.2 0.025 280); /* elevated surface (hovered cards) */"
                - generic [ref=e225]: "--text-primary: oklch(0.95 0.008 280); /* main body text — 15.05:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e227]: "--text-secondary: oklch(0.77 0.015 280); /* secondary text — 4.50:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e229]: "--text-muted: oklch(0.72 0.02 280); /* muted / meta text — 3.28:1 contrast */"
                - generic [ref=e233]: "--brand-primary: oklch(0.64 0.16 75); /* Primary Amber */"
                - generic [ref=e235]: "--brand-accent: oklch(0.58 0.18 35); /* Accent Coral */"
                - generic [ref=e237]: "--brand-highlight: oklch(0.81 0.15 110); /* Highlight Chartreuse */"
                - generic [ref=e239]: "--neutral-anchor: oklch(0.53 0.08 230); /* Anchor Slate */"
                - generic [ref=e241]: "--accent-cold: oklch(0.69 0.15 250); /* Interaction Cerulean */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e243] [cursor=pointer]
            - heading "Light Theme Override" [level=3] [ref=e245]
            - paragraph [ref=e246]: "The light theme is triggered by either the attribute selector or the system preference media query, and overrides only the color tokens. Typography and spacing stay the same:"
            - figure [ref=e248]:
              - code [ref=e250]:
                - generic [ref=e252]: "[data-theme=\"light\"] {"
                - generic [ref=e254]: "--bg-main: oklch(0.985 0.003 280); /* near-white background */"
                - generic [ref=e256]: "--bg-surface: oklch(1 0 0); /* pure white cards */"
                - generic [ref=e258]: "--bg-surface-elevated: oklch(0.92 0.008 280);"
                - generic [ref=e262]: "--text-primary: oklch(0.13 0.015 280);"
                - generic [ref=e264]: "--text-secondary: oklch(0.25 0.02 280);"
                - generic [ref=e266]: "--text-muted: oklch(0.38 0.025 280);"
                - generic [ref=e270]: "--brand-primary: oklch(0.55 0.15 75); /* Rich Amber */"
                - generic [ref=e272]: "--brand-accent: oklch(0.48 0.16 35); /* Deep Coral */"
                - generic [ref=e274]: "--brand-highlight: oklch(0.45 0.14 110); /* High-Contrast Chartreuse */"
                - generic [ref=e276]: "--neutral-anchor: oklch(0.35 0.06 230); /* Dark Slate Anchor */"
                - generic [ref=e278]: "--accent-cold: oklch(0.52 0.14 250); /* Deep Interactive Cerulean */"
                - generic [ref=e280]: "}"
                - generic [ref=e284]: "@media (prefers-color-scheme: light) {"
                - generic [ref=e286]: ":root { /* identical values */ }"
                - generic [ref=e288]: "}"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e290] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e292]: Using OKLCH ensures that the light and dark themes stay perceptually consistent — the same hue angles produce recognizably the same color families across themes, differing only in lightness. Every text token on both themes exceeds WCAG AA contrast against its background.
            - heading "Border Radii" [level=2] [ref=e293]
            - figure [ref=e295]:
              - code [ref=e297]:
                - generic [ref=e299]: "--radius-sm: 4px;"
                - generic [ref=e301]: "--radius-md: 8px;"
                - generic [ref=e303]: "--radius-lg: 12px;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e305] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e307]:
              - text: A tight radius system. Cards use
              - code [ref=e308]: "--radius-lg"
              - text: ", buttons and inputs use"
              - code [ref=e309]: "--radius-md"
              - text: ", code blocks use"
              - code [ref=e310]: "--radius-sm"
              - text: .
            - heading "Motion" [level=2] [ref=e311]
            - figure [ref=e313]:
              - code [ref=e315]:
                - generic [ref=e317]: "--transition-fast: 0.15s ease-out;"
                - generic [ref=e319]: "--transition-normal: 0.25s ease-out;"
                - generic [ref=e321]: "--transition-slow: 0.4s ease-out;"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e323] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e325]:
              - text: All transitions use
              - code [ref=e326]: ease-out
              - text: — fast in, slow out — which feels responsive because the motion starts immediately and coasts to rest. The
              - code [ref=e327]: "prefers-reduced-motion: reduce"
              - text: media query disables all transitions and animations.
            - heading "Structural Tokens" [level=2] [ref=e328]
            - figure [ref=e330]:
              - code [ref=e332]:
                - generic [ref=e334]: "--size-border-pixel: 2px;"
                - generic [ref=e336]: "--size-border-thick: 4px;"
                - generic [ref=e338]: "--size-touch-target-min: 44px;"
                - generic [ref=e342]: "--layout-max-width: 68ch; /* comfortable reading measure */"
                - generic [ref=e344]: "--layout-max-width-wide: 75rem; /* full page content area */"
                - generic [ref=e346]: "--bp-mobile: 720px; /* mobile breakpoint */"
              - button "Copy to clipboard" [ref=e348] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e350]:
              - text: The 68-character line length for body text follows the established range of 45–75 characters per line. The touch target minimum of 44px matches
              - link "WCAG 2.1 Success Criterion 2.5.8" [ref=e351] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG21/#target-size
              - text: .

              - text: css

                - /url: /blog/css-grid-layouts/
            - paragraph [ref=e203]: Subgrid, auto-fill vs auto-fit, grid areas for responsive dashboards, and when not to use Grid at all.
            - paragraph [ref=e204]:
              - strong [ref=e205]: Eric Carlisle
              - time [ref=e206]: October 14, 2022
              - generic [ref=e207]: ·1 min read

              - link "css" [ref=e209] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: /tags/css/
      - navigation "Pagination" [ref=e210]:
        - link "Next page →" [ref=e212] [cursor=pointer]:
          - /url: /blog/page/2
          - generic [ref=e213]: Next page →
  - contentinfo [ref=e214]:

    - text: © 2026 Eric Carlisle. All rights reserved.

            - paragraph [ref=e59]: This article documents every schema graph that ships to search engines, so you know exactly what structured data is available for indexing, rich results, and knowledge graph connections.

            - paragraph [ref=e564]:
              - text: All types also render Open Graph, Twitter Card, and standard meta tags via
              - code [ref=e565]: BaseHead.astro
              - text: . The full
              - code [ref=e566]: "@graph"
              - text: is rendered inside a single
              - code [ref=e567]: <script type="application/ld+json">
              - text: element, placed just before
              - code [ref=e568]: </head>
              - text: .
            - heading "Debugging Structured Data" [level=2] [ref=e569]
            - paragraph [ref=e570]: "To inspect the live schema on any page:"
            - list [ref=e571]:
              - listitem [ref=e572]:
                - text: Open
                - strong [ref=e573]: Chrome DevTools → Elements
                - text: and search for
                - code [ref=e574]: ld+json
                - text: .
              - listitem [ref=e575]:
                - text: Use
                - strong [ref=e576]:
                  - text: Google’s
                  - link "Rich Results Test" [ref=e577] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: https://search.google.com/test/rich-results
                - text: to validate the markup.
              - listitem [ref=e578]:
                - text: Use
                - strong [ref=e579]:
                  - text: Schema.org’s
                  - link "Validator" [ref=e580] [cursor=pointer]:
                    - /url: https://validator.schema.org/
                - text: to trace
                - code [ref=e581]: "@id"
                - text: references and verify entity relationships.
            - paragraph [ref=e582]:
              - text: Every article page should pass the Rich Results Test with zero errors. If it doesn’t, the most common cause is a missing or invalid
              - code [ref=e583]: heroImage
              - text: path, since
              - code [ref=e584]: image
              - text: is always included in the
              - code [ref=e585]: BlogPosting
              - text: entity.

## AGENTS.md

```md
# AGENTS.md

> Claude Code users: see [CLAUDE.md](./CLAUDE.md) for full guidance.

## Project

Astro 6 static blog — Markdown/MDX content collections, RSS, sitemap, local fonts.

- Package manager: **pnpm** (required — do not use npm/yarn)
- Node: `>=22.12.0`

## Commands

```sh
pnpm dev            # dev server
pnpm build          # production build
pnpm preview        # preview build output
pnpm astro check    # type-check .astro files
pnpm exec vitest    # run tests
pnpm exec biome check .           # lint
pnpm exec biome format . --write  # format
```

No `lint`/`test`/`format` package scripts — always use `pnpm exec` directly.

## Key Files

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `astro.config.mjs` | Integrations (MDX, sitemap), local font config |
| `src/content.config.ts` | **Authoritative** blog frontmatter schema (Zod) |
| `src/styles/global.css` | Design tokens — use these, not hardcoded values |
| `src/styles/components.css` | Shared component helpers (`.card`, `.tag`, `.callout`, …) |
| `src/layouts/BlogPost.astro` | Wraps every post; hero image, metadata, header/footer |
| `src/components/BaseHead.astro` | Canonical URL, OG/Twitter tags, font loading |
| `docs/design_system_specification.md` | Token naming reference |

## Architecture

- **Routing**: file-based under `src/pages/`; `[...slug].astro` renders posts
- **Content**: `getCollection('blog')` from `src/content/blog/**/*.{md,mdx}`
- **RSS/Sitemap**: auto-generated by integrations; no manual maintenance needed

## Design Rules

- Dark mode is the **default**; light mode activates via `html[data-theme="light"]`
- Use CSS custom properties from `global.css` — no hardcoded colors, px/rem when tokens exist
- Fluid spacing tokens: `--space-xs` / `--space-sm` / `--space-md` / `--space-lg` / `--space-xl`
- Respect `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` for animations
- 44px minimum touch targets; preserve `:focus-visible` on interactive elements

## Non-Obvious Pitfalls

1. **Docs ≠ live tokens**: `design_system_specification.md` describes `--step-1` / `--step-2` naming; `global.css` uses `--space-xs`/`--space-sm` naming. Match `global.css` when editing styles.
2. **Site URL mismatch**: `astro.config.mjs` sets `site: 'https://example.com'`; `vitest.config.ts` overrides to `https://ericcarlisle.com`. Don't rely on `Astro.site` in tests.
3. **Frontmatter schema**: Use `src/content.config.ts` as the single source of truth — the docs contain an outdated snippet.
4. **No theme toggle component**: `data-theme="light"` switching exists in CSS but no UI component implements it yet.

```

---

## astro.config.mjs

```js
// @ts-check

import mdx from '@astrojs/mdx';
import partytown from '@astrojs/partytown';
import sitemap from '@astrojs/sitemap';
import compress from '@playform/compress';
import { defineConfig } from 'astro/config';
import expressiveCode from 'astro-expressive-code';
import pagefind from 'astro-pagefind';

// https://astro.build/config
export default defineConfig({
  site: 'https://ericcarlisle.com',
  integrations: [
    partytown({
      config: {
        forward: ['dataLayer.push'],
      },
    }),
    expressiveCode({
      themes: ['dark-plus'],
      styleOverrides: {
        codeFontFamily: 'var(--font-mono)',
        uiFontFamily: 'var(--font-copy)',
        borderRadius: 'var(--radius-md)',
        frames: {
          editorTabBarBackground: 'oklch(0.18 0.025 280)',
          editorActiveTabBackground: 'oklch(0.12 0.015 280)',
          terminalBackground: 'oklch(0.12 0.015 280)',
        },
      },
      defaultProps: {
        overridesByLang: {
          bash: { frame: 'terminal' },
          shell: { frame: 'terminal' },
        },
      },
    }),
    mdx(),
    pagefind(),
    sitemap(),
    compress(),
  ],
  vite: {
    resolve: {
      alias: {
        '@styles': '/src/styles',
        '@images': '/src/assets/images',
        '@components': '/src/components',
        '@lib': '/src/lib',
      },
    },
  },
});

```

{
  "$schema": "https://biomejs.dev/schemas/2.5.0/schema.json",
  "formatter": {
    "enabled": true,
    "formatWithErrors": false,
    "indentStyle": "space",
    "indentWidth": 2,
    "lineWidth": 100,
    "lineEnding": "lf"
  },

    "enabled": true,
    "rules": {
      "preset": "recommended",
      "style": {
        "useConst": "error"
      },
      "correctness": {
        "noUnusedVariables": "off",
        "noUnusedImports": "off"
      },
      "complexity": {
        "noImportantStyles": "off"
      }
    }
  },
  "javascript": {
    "formatter": {
      "quoteStyle": "single",
      "trailingCommas": "all",
      "semicolons": "always"
    }
  },
  "vcs": {
    "enabled": true,
    "clientKind": "git",
    "useIgnoreFile": true
  }
}

{
  "name": "ericcarlisle-contact-worker",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "wrangler dev",
    "deploy": "wrangler deploy",
    "deploy:prod": "wrangler deploy --env production",
    "deploy:dev": "wrangler deploy --env development",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "secrets": "wrangler secret put TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY && wrangler secret put RESEND_API_KEY && wrangler secret put RESEND_FROM_EMAIL"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cloudflare/workers-types": "^4.20250401.0",
    "typescript": "^5.8.0",
    "wrangler": "^4.0.0"
  }
}

```

---

## contact-worker/src/index.ts

```ts
export interface Env {
  TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY: string;
  RESEND_API_KEY: string;
  RESEND_FROM_EMAIL: string;
}

interface ContactBody {
  name: string;
  email: string;
  subject: string;
  message: string;
  botField: string;
  timestamp: number;
  turnstileToken: string;
}

function json(data: Record<string, unknown>, status: number) {
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data), {
    status,
    headers: {
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
    },
  });
}

function str(val: unknown): string {
  return val == null ? '' : String(val);
}

function parseBody(raw: unknown): ContactBody {
  const body = raw as Record<string, unknown>;
  return {
    name: str(body.name).trim(),
    email: str(body.email).trim(),
    subject: str(body.subject).trim(),
    message: str(body.message).trim(),
    botField: str(body.botField),
    timestamp: Number(str(body.ts)) || 0,
    turnstileToken: str(body['cf-turnstile-response']),
  };
}

function escapeHtml(s: string): string {
  return s
    .replace(/&/g, '&amp;')
    .replace(/</g, '&lt;')
    .replace(/>/g, '&gt;')
    .replace(/"/g, '&quot;')
    .replace(/'/g, '&#x27;');
}

const rateLimitMap = new Map<string, { count: number; resetAt: number }>();
const RATE_LIMIT_MAX = 5;
const RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW = 60_000;

function checkRateLimit(ip: string): boolean {
  const now = Date.now();
  const entry = rateLimitMap.get(ip);
  if (!entry || now > entry.resetAt) {
    rateLimitMap.set(ip, { count: 1, resetAt: now + RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW });
    return true;
  }
  if (entry.count >= RATE_LIMIT_MAX) return false;
  entry.count++;
  return true;
}

function allFilled(...values: string[]): boolean {
  return values.every((v) => v.length > 0);
}

function validate(body: ContactBody, ip: string): Response | null {
  if (!allFilled(body.name, body.email, body.subject, body.message)) {
    return json({ error: 'All fields are required.' }, 400);
  }
  if (body.botField) {
    return json({ error: 'Bad request' }, 400);
  }
  if (Date.now() - body.timestamp < 3000) {
    return json({ error: 'Too fast – possible bot' }, 429);
  }
  if (!checkRateLimit(ip)) {
    return json({ error: 'Too many requests. Please try again later.' }, 429);
  }
  return null;
}

async function verifyTurnstile(
  token: string,
  secret: string,
  ip: string,
): Promise<Response | null> {
  const resp = await fetch('https://challenges.cloudflare.com/turnstile/v0/siteverify', {
    method: 'POST',
    body: new URLSearchParams({ secret, response: token, remoteip: ip }),
  });

  const result = (await resp.json()) as { success: boolean };
  if (!result.success) {
    console.warn('Turnstile verification failed', result);
    return json({ error: 'CAPTCHA verification failed' }, 400);
  }
  return null;
}

async function sendEmail(body: ContactBody, env: Env): Promise<Response | null> {
  const { RESEND_API_KEY: resendKey, RESEND_FROM_EMAIL: fromEmail } = env;

  const name = escapeHtml(body.name);
  const email = escapeHtml(body.email);
  const escapedMessage = escapeHtml(body.message).replace(/\n/g, '<br/>');

  const resp = await fetch('https://api.resend.com/emails', {
    method: 'POST',
    headers: {
      Authorization: `Bearer ${resendKey}`,
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    },
    body: JSON.stringify({
      from: fromEmail,
      to: fromEmail,
      subject: `[Contact] ${body.subject}`,
      reply_to: body.email,
      html: `
        <p><strong>Name:</strong> ${name}</p>
        <p><strong>Email:</strong> ${email}</p>
        <p><strong>Message:</strong></p>
        <p>${escapedMessage}</p>
      `,
    }),
  });

  if (!resp.ok) {
    const errText = await resp.text();
    console.error('Resend email failed', errText);
    return json({ error: 'Failed to send message' }, 500);
  }

  return null;
}

export default {
  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
    if (request.method === 'OPTIONS') {
      return new Response(null, {
        headers: {
          'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*',
          'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'POST, OPTIONS',
          'Access-Control-Allow-Headers': 'Content-Type',
          'Access-Control-Max-Age': '86400',
        },
      });
    }

    if (request.method !== 'POST') {
      return json({ error: 'Method not allowed' }, 405);
    }

    const ip = request.headers.get('CF-Connecting-IP') ?? 'unknown';

    let raw: unknown;
    try {
      raw = await request.json();
    } catch {
      return json({ error: 'Invalid JSON' }, 400);
    }

    const body = parseBody(raw);

    const validationError = validate(body, ip);
    if (validationError) return validationError;

    const turnstileError = await verifyTurnstile(body.turnstileToken, env.TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY, ip);
    if (turnstileError) return turnstileError;

    const emailError = await sendEmail(body, env);
    if (emailError) return emailError;

    return json({ ok: true }, 200);
  },
};

```

## docs/author_profile.md

```md
# Author Profile

This document serves as the authoritative source of truth for the author's identity, personal website, and official social media channels. AI agents must reference these links when generating site metadata, footers, headers, or social sharing components. Do not use template placeholders or default Astro social accounts.

## Identity & Biography

* **Full Name**: Eric Carlisle
* **Job Title**: Principal Full-Stack Engineer & UX Architect
* **Organization**: Eric Carlisle Consulting
* **Primary Domain**: https://ericcarlisle.com
* **Avatar Image Asset**: https://ericcarlisle.com/eric-carlisle.webp
* **Author Schema ID**: `https://ericcarlisle.com/#person`

## Verified Social Channels

| Platform | Official Profile URL |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Personal Website** | [ericcarlisle.com](https://ericcarlisle.com) |
| **GitHub** | [github.com/ecarlisle](https://github.com/ecarlisle) |
| **LinkedIn** | [linkedin.com/in/ericcarlisle](https://www.linkedin.com/in/ericcarlisle/) |
| **Bluesky** | [bsky.app/profile/ericcarlisle.bsky.social](https://bsky.app/profile/ericcarlisle.bsky.social) |
| **Mastodon** | [fosstodon.org/@ericcarlisle](https://fosstodon.org/@ericcarlisle) |

---

## Machine Usage Rules

1. **Social Sharing Components**: When rendering share links for articles, use the platform URLs explicitly listed above.
2. **Metadata Injection**: Use `https://ericcarlisle.com` as the canonical base URL for all generated RSS feeds, sitemaps, and Open Graph/Twitter card absolute image references.
3. **Structured Data (JSON-LD)**: When generating the `Person` block inside `SchemaOrg.astro`, map the `sameAs` array, `jobTitle`, and `worksFor` properties strictly to the metadata provided above, ensuring the profile links directly back to the static `@id: "https://ericcarlisle.com/#person"`.

```

## docs/todo.md

```md
# AstroBlog — TODO: "Ready for Content" Checklist

## 🔴 Critical Gaps (Blocking "Ready for Content")

- [ ] **Contact page** — Create `/contact` route (referenced in `BaseHead` types, needed for lead gen & WCAG 2.4.6)
- [ ] **Tags index page** — Create `/tags/` page listing all tags with counts (discovery, SEO taxonomy, UX)
- [ ] **Newsletter/email capture** — Add signup form + integration (ConvertKit/Buttondown/Beehiiv) for audience ownership
- [ ] **Cookie consent / privacy banner** — GA4 via Partytown requires GDPR/CCPA consent before tracking

---

## 🟡 High-Impact UX/SEO

### Article Enhancements
- [ ] **Author box on articles** — Mini bio + links (E-E-A-T signal, personal brand) — currently only on `/about`
- [ ] **Related posts section** — Content graph based on tags/embeddings (increases dwell time, internal linking)
- [ ] **Reading progress label** — Add percentage label to progress bar (WCAG 1.3.1)

### Content Metadata
- [ ] **Enforce `updatedDate`** — Add to frontmatter schema validation + CI check for freshness signals
- [ ] **Per-post social images (OG)** — Replace fallback with generated/designed images per post (social CTR)
- [ ] **Series/part support** — Frontmatter field + UI for multi-part articles

### Technical SEO
- [ ] **Full-content RSS** — Current feed truncates; offer full content option for power users
- [ ] **Webmentions/IndieWeb** — Receive/display mentions (decentralized comments)
- [ ] **JSON Feed** — Alternative to RSS for modern readers

---

## 🟢 Readability/Polish

- [ ] **Verify reduced motion** — Ensure `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` wraps all animations in `global.css`
- [ ] **Syntax highlighting theme switching** — Add light/dark theme toggle for code blocks (Expressive Code supports this)
- [ ] **Copy code button** — Verify Expressive Code copy button works (should be enabled by default)
- [ ] **Line numbers toggle** — Optional line numbers for code blocks
- [ ] **Anchor links on headings** — Hover-reveal `#` links for deep linking (currently TOC only)
- [ ] **Print stylesheet** — Clean print layout for articles

---

## 📋 Marketing/Growth

- [ ] **Comments system** — Giscus (GitHub Discussions) or Utterances (GitHub Issues) or self-hosted
- [ ] **Newsletter archive page** — `/newsletter` with past issues (if using Buttondown/Beehiiv)
- [ ] **Social proof** — Testimonials, subscriber count, "featured in" logos
- [ ] **Lead magnets** — PDF guides, checklists gated behind email
- [ ] **UTM tracking** — Auto-append UTM params to outbound links for attribution
- [ ] **Performance monitoring** — Lighthouse CI in GitHub Actions (budget enforcement)

---

## 🏗️ Infrastructure/DevEx

- [ ] **CI/CD pipeline** — GitHub Actions: typecheck → lint → build → deploy preview
- [ ] **Dependabot/Renovate** — Automated dependency updates
- [ ] **Visual regression** — Playwright + pixelmatch or Chromatic for design system
- [ ] **Edge middleware** — Geo-based redirects, A/B testing, auth preview
- [ ] **Image CDN** — Cloudinary/Imgix for hero images (auto-format, responsive)

---

## 📝 Content Operations

- [ ] **Editorial calendar** — Notion/Airtable/GitHub Projects integration
- [ ] **Draft/preview workflow** — `draft: true` frontmatter + preview deployment
- [ ] **Content audit script** — Check for: broken links, missing alt text, short descriptions, stale content
- [ ] **Style guide enforcement** — Vale/lint-md for prose consistency (terminology, tone, formatting)

---

## 🎯 Priority Order (Suggested)

| Phase | Items | Effort |
|-------|-------|--------|
| **1. Complete IA** | Contact page, Tags index | Low |
| **2. Authority Signals** | Author box on articles, updatedDate enforcement | Low-Med |
| **3. Growth Foundation** | Newsletter capture, Cookie consent | Med |
| **4. Engagement** | Related posts, Comments, Full RSS | Med |
| **5. Social/SEO** | Per-post OG images, Webmentions | Med-High |
| **6. Infrastructure** | CI/CD, Visual regression, Performance budgets | High |
| **7. Content Ops** | Editorial calendar, Draft workflow, Content audit | Ongoing |

---

## ✅ Already Done (Verified)

- [x] Astro 6 + TypeScript + vanilla CSS (OKLCH tokens)
- [x] Pagefind full-text search
- [x] Partytown for GA4 (third-party script off main thread)
- [x] Expressive Code syntax highlighting (dark-plus theme)
- [x] JSON-LD structured data (BlogPosting, CollectionPage, BreadcrumbList)
- [x] Open Graph + Twitter Cards
- [x] Sitemap + RSS feed
- [x] Dark mode (localStorage pre-paint, no flash)
- [x] Skip links, focus-visible, 44px touch targets
-18px targets
- [x] Reading progress bar + TOC (client-side, sticky)
- [x] Tag filtering (client-side, URL-synced)
- [x] Pagination (Astro native)
- [x] Image optimization (`<Image>` + Sharp)
- [x] 404 page with personality
- [x] About page with stats grid
- [x] Style guide page
- [x] Dead code eliminated (fallow clean)
- [x] Duplication refactored (shared components: TagFilterBar, PostGrid, blog-utils)

---

*Generated from audit on 2026-06-19. Update as items are completed.*
```

## lighthouserc.js

```js
export default {
  ci: {
    collect: {
      startServerCommand: 'npm run preview',
      url: ['http://localhost:4321/'],
      numberOfRuns: 3,
    },
    assert: {
      assertions: {
        'categories:performance': ['error', { minScore: 0.95 }],
        'categories:accessibility': ['error', { minScore: 0.95 }],
        'categories:seo': ['error', { minScore: 1.0 }],
        'categories:best-practices': ['error', { minScore: 0.95 }],
      },
    },
  },
};

```

---

## pnpm-workspace.yaml

```yaml
allowBuilds:
  esbuild: true
  sharp: true

```

---

## README.txt

```txt
Eric Carlisle — AstroBlog
==========================

Personal blog at https://ericcarlisle.com. Built with Astro 6 static site
generation. Technical deep-dives on web performance, accessibility, React,
and modern CSS architecture.


Stack
-----

Framework:  Astro 6.4 (SSG, output: "static")
Language:   TypeScript 6.0
Styling:    Vanilla CSS with OKLCH design tokens — no Tailwind
Package:    pnpm (>=22.12.0 Node)
Linting:    Biome 2.5 (single quotes, trailing commas, semicolons)
Analysis:   fallow (dead code, complexity, plugin)
Typecheck:  astro check (via @astrojs/check)
Testing:    Vitest installed, not used


Project Structure
-----------------

src/
  content/
    blog/             .mdx posts, 20+ articles
    [content.config.ts](src/content.config.ts)  Zod schema for frontmatter validation
  [components/](src/components/)       14 Astro components (reusable UI)
  [layouts/](src/layouts/)
    [BlogPost.astro](src/layouts/BlogPost.astro)   Single shared layout (articles, about, style-guide)
  lib/
    [blog-utils.ts](src/lib/blog-utils.ts)     Tag taxonomy, post sorting
    [word-count.ts](src/lib/word-count.ts)     Word-count utilities (extracted from blog-utils)
    [webmentions.ts](src/lib/webmentions.ts)   Webmention.io client
  [pages/](src/pages/)             Route pages (index, blog, contact, tags, 404, about)
  styles/
    [global.css](src/styles/global.css)         Design tokens, typography, base layout
    [components.css](src/styles/components.css) Shared component styles
    [feed.css](src/styles/feed.css)             Blog feed list styles
    [pagefind.css](src/styles/pagefind.css)     Search UI overrides
  [assets/images/](src/assets/images/)     Hero images, avatars, OG images
    [consts.ts](src/consts.ts)                 Site-wide constants (author, social, analytics)

[contact-worker/](contact-worker/)      Cloudflare Worker (contact form API)
    [src/index.ts](contact-worker/src/index.ts) POST handler (validation, Turnstile, Resend email)
    [wrangler.toml](contact-worker/wrangler.toml) Deployment config
    [package.json](contact-worker/package.json) Worker dependencies


Architecture Highlights
-----------------------

Layout: Single [BlogPost.astro](src/layouts/BlogPost.astro) shared by all pages. Article-only features
  (TOC, reading progress bar, dates, pagination, tags) gated behind
  {pageType === 'article' && ...}.

Content: Posts are .mdx in [src/content/blog/](src/content/blog/) with Zod-validated frontmatter
  via [src/content.config.ts](src/content.config.ts). pubDate uses z.coerce.date() for fuzzy
  locale date parsing. description is capped at 165 characters.

Design Tokens: All metrics and colors live in [src/styles/global.css](src/styles/global.css) as CSS
  custom properties in OKLCH color space. No px/rem hard-coded anywhere.

Accessibility: WCAG AA compliant. Minimum 44px touch targets, dashed
  :focus-visible outlines, prefers-reduced-motion wrappers on animations.
  No --text-muted on --bg-surface-elevated (contrast failure guard).

Search: [Pagefind](https://pagefind.app/) indexes all static HTML at build time via [astro-pagefind](https://github.com/shishkin/astro-pagefind).

Analytics: Google Analytics 4 via [Partytown](https://partytown.builder.io/) (offloaded to web worker).

SEO: Sitemap ([@astrojs/sitemap](https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/integrations-guide/sitemap/)), RSS feed ([@astrojs/rss](https://docs.astro.build/en/guides/rss/)), JSON-LD structured
  data ([SchemaOrg.astro](src/components/SchemaOrg.astro) with BlogPosting + Person). Open Graph and Twitter
  Card metadata via [BaseHead.astro](src/components/BaseHead.astro).

Webmentions: Fetched from webmention.io at build time via WEBMENTION_IO_TOKEN
  env var (set in [.env.production](.env.production)). Brid.gy forwards social engagement
  from Bluesky and Mastodon.

Contact Form: POST handler lives in [contact-worker/](contact-worker/) as a separate Cloudflare
  Worker. Validates input, checks Turnstile CAPTCHA, rate limits, sends
  email via Resend API. Frontend in [contact.astro](src/pages/contact.astro) uses PUBLIC_CONTACT_API_URL
  env variable to point at worker URL (localhost:8787 in dev).

Tag Filtering: Client-side inline script via [TagFilterBar.astro](src/components/TagFilterBar.astro)
  on index/blog pages. Tag pages (/tags/[tag]) exist as static routes but are
  empty — filtering is done in-browser.

Reading Time: Server-side from word count (Math.ceil(wordCount / 200)).

Code Highlighting: Expressive Code via [astro-expressive-code](https://expressive-code.com/)
  with dark-plus theme and terminal frames for shell blocks.

Image Optimization: [Sharp](https://sharp.pixelplumbing.com/) compresses WebP hero images at build time
  via [@playform/compress](https://github.com/PlayForm/Compress).


CLI Commands
------------

pnpm dev              Dev server on localhost:4321
pnpm build            Build to ./dist/ (includes Pagefind indexing)
pnpm typecheck        pnpm astro check
pnpm lint             pnpm exec biome check .
pnpm format           pnpm exec biome format . --write
pnpm fallow:dead-code npx fallow dead-code --unused-files --unused-deps
pnpm fallow:audit     npx fallow audit --changed-since main
pnpm lighthouse:all   node scripts/lighthouse-all.mjs


Environment Files
-----------------

[.env.development](.env.development)          Dev vars (Turnstile test key, localhost worker URL)
[.env.production](.env.production)            Production vars (real keys, deployed worker URL)
.env.local            Local overrides (gitignored, not committed)
[.env.example](.env.example)                  Template for reference

Secrets for the Cloudflare Worker are set via [wrangler secret put](https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/secrets/), not in env files.


Deployment
----------

Astro:     Static site output to ./dist/. Deploy to any static host.
Worker:    cd [contact-worker](contact-worker/) && wrangler deploy --env production.
           Secrets: wrangler secret put TURNSTILE_SECRET_KEY
                    wrangler secret put RESEND_API_KEY
                    wrangler secret put RESEND_FROM_EMAIL

API domain should be set via PUBLIC_CONTACT_API_URL in [.env.production](.env.production)
  and added to Cloudflare DNS if using a custom subdomain.

```
